Consular Scanning and the PDI Biometric Matching Protocol

Chilean consulates are equipped with advanced document verification technology that extends beyond simple visual inspection. When your visa application is processed, the consular officer scans the physical 45x45mm photo using a high-speed document scanner. The image is not simply attached to a file; it is run through a software module licensed by the PDI's Departamento de Extranjería y Migración. This software analyzes the photo's geometry against the strict 60% head height requirement. Here is the technical breakdown of why the 60% figure is non-negotiable for visas:

  • Interoperabilidad con PDI (Interoperability with PDI): The visa photo is the foundational record for your identity in the Chilean immigration system. When you later apply for a Cédula de Identidad para Extranjeros (Identity Card for Foreigners), the photo on file from your visa application is used as the baseline comparison. If the visa photo was poorly proportioned, the system may reject the new photo capture for the Cédula, requiring you to visit an office of the Registro Civil with a corrected photo, causing administrative delays.
  • Prevención de Suplantación de Identidad (Identity Fraud Prevention): By standardizing the head size to exactly 60%, the Chilean system prevents fraudsters from using photo collages or manipulated images. In a 45x45mm square with a fixed 27mm head, there is a predictable amount of white space around the face. Any attempt to digitally alter the background or the facial features disrupts this spatial ratio, alerting the consular officer to potential tampering.
  • Lectura en Puertas Automáticas (Automated Gate Reading): The Control Migratorio Automatizado at Santiago Airport uses a fixed-focus camera. The algorithm expects a specific eye-line height and interpupillary distance relative to the head size. By ensuring the head is exactly 60% of the frame, the visa photo provides the algorithm with the exact scaling factor needed to match the face to the live traveler standing at the gate.

Lista de Verificación Esencial para Solicitudes de Visa a Chile

To prevent your visa application from being returned with the dreaded stamp "Fotografía No Cumple Normativa" (Photo Does Not Meet Standards), adhere to the following checklist derived from the official Circular N° 12 of the Chilean Ministry of Foreign Affairs regarding consular photo standards:

  • Expresión Facial (Facial Expression): The face must be captured with a completely neutral expression. The mouth must be closed, and the eyes must be open and looking directly at the camera lens. The face must be fully visible from the chin to the crown of the head. For male applicants, beards are permitted but must represent the applicant's current, daily appearance. A photo with a full beard followed by an appearance at the consulate clean-shaven will cause a mismatch during the interview process.
  • Iluminación (Lighting): The background must be an even, uniform white. The consular scanning process is particularly unforgiving of shadows on the neck and jawline. Because the 45x45mm square includes the subject's shoulders and upper torso, the lighting must be balanced to avoid a "floating head" effect where the white shirt blends into the white background. A subtle but distinct collar line is required for proper orientation of the square image.
  • Accesorios (Accessories): Glasses must be removed. Chilean consulates adhere strictly to the ICAO recommendation for unhindered eye visibility for visa photos. This is because the visa photo is used for pre-flight background checks and facial recognition against Interpol databases. Tinted lenses or thick frames that obscure the eyelids or eye sockets will result in immediate rejection. Head coverings for religious reasons are permitted only with a signed declaration and must not cast shadows on the face.
  • Formato del Archivo Digital (Digital File Format): For the online visa application portal (Tramites en Línea), the digital upload must be a JPEG file with dimensions of exactly 1063 x 1063 pixels. The file size should be kept under 2MB. Uploading a photo with a lower resolution (e.g., 800x800) will cause the portal to stretch the image, distorting the 60% head ratio and resulting in a pixelated, rejected submission.

Solicitud de Visa Sin Errores: Evite la Devolución de Antecedentes

The bureaucratic process for obtaining a Chilean visa can be complex, requiring apostilled documents, proof of funds, and a police clearance certificate. Having your application paused or returned due to a simple photo error is a costly and frustrating setback. The consular fee is non-refundable, and the delay can jeopardize travel plans or employment start dates. By utilizing our specialized photo tool, you eliminate the most common variable of rejection. Our algorithm provides a photo that is geometrically locked to the 45x45mm square and the 60% head ratio. When the consular officer places the biometric overlay on your printed photo, the alignment will be exact. When the PDI system scans your visa file at the border, the biometric handshake will be instantaneous. This precision ensures that your interaction with Chilean immigration begins and ends with efficiency, allowing you to focus on the purpose of your journey rather than the minutiae of passport photo compliance.

Digital Upload for E-Visa and Consular Portals

Chile has significantly modernized its visa application process through the Sistema de Atención Consular Virtual. For nationals of countries that require a Visto de Turismo Simple, the application is often submitted entirely online. The digital portal is configured to perform an automated pre-validation of the uploaded photo. The algorithm checks the file header for the pixel dimensions (1063x1063) and performs a quick facial detection scan. If the system cannot detect a face occupying roughly 60% of the frame, it will generate an error message preventing the applicant from proceeding to the payment step. Our digital download is specifically formatted to bypass this automated check. The file is saved with the correct color profile (sRGB) and without EXIF metadata that might inflate the file size. This ensures a smooth upload to the Portal de Trámites Consulares, allowing your application to move immediately to the queue for human review by a consular officer. By ensuring the photo meets the strict technical and biometric standards of the Chilean Ministry of Foreign Affairs, you set the foundation for a successful and timely visa approval.

Chinese Passport Photo Requirements 2026 — Official 33×48mm Specifications

The Exit and Entry Administration of the Ministry of Public Security (MPS) of the People's Republic of China sets strict biometric photo standards for all Chinese passport applications. The required photo size is 33mm wide × 48mm high — a unique dimension that is narrower and taller than the international 35×45mm standard used across Europe, and completely different from the 51×51mm (2×2 inch) format used in the United States. Submitting any other size will result in immediate rejection at the Public Security Bureau (PSB) counter.

Our tool automatically crops your photo to the exact 33×48mm frame, outputs a 826×1200 pixel JPEG at 300 DPI, and positions your face so that head height falls within the required 58% to 69% of the total image height — the tight biometric zone mandated by Chinese scanning equipment.

Why the 33×48mm Size Is Different — And Why It Matters

The 33×48mm format is not interchangeable with any other country's standard. It is approximately 2mm narrower than a European biometric photo and 3mm shorter than the 51mm height of a US passport photo. These differences are not cosmetic — Chinese PSB counters and online submission portals run automated dimensional checks that reject photos outside the accepted range. Simply trimming a European or American photo to approximate these dimensions will also change the head-to-frame ratio, pushing it outside the 58–69% head height requirement.

The MPS's 58–69% head height range is also tighter than the ICAO's broader 70–80% recommendation used by most Western countries. This means a photo cropped for a UK or US passport will typically show too much of the head relative to the frame and will fail Chinese biometric verification. Our AI facial-landmark detection places the chin, eye line, and crown precisely within the compliant zone before generating the final file.

Complete Chinese Passport Photo Compliance Checklist

Background

The background must be pure white — not off-white, cream, or light grey. The MPS specifies that the background must be uniform with no patterns, gradients, or shadows. White or very light-coloured clothing should be avoided specifically because it blends into the white background, making it impossible for the PSB's border-detection software to distinguish the applicant's outline from the background.

Facial Expression and Head Position

A neutral expression is required. The mouth must be fully closed — even a slight open-mouthed smile that does not show teeth can cause the application to be flagged. The head must face the camera directly, with both eyes fully open and looking straight at the lens. The MPS requires both ears to be visible where possible. Long hair that covers the ears should be pinned back. The forehead must be unobstructed — no hair falling across the brow line.

Lighting

Lighting must be even and shadow-free across both the face and the background. The MPS uses high-contrast imaging to detect even faint shadows on the neck, under the chin, or across the cheek. Photos taken in uneven indoor lighting — particularly with a single overhead light source — frequently fail this check. Our tool detects and corrects lighting imbalances automatically.

Clothing

Dark-coloured clothing is strongly preferred. White, off-white, and very light-coloured tops must be avoided as they cause the automated background-detection algorithm to fail. Uniforms that resemble military or government attire are prohibited. There is no requirement to wear formal clothing — plain, dark casual clothing is fully acceptable.

Eyeglasses

Eyeglasses are strictly prohibited in Chinese passport photos. This applies to all types of glasses including prescription, sunglasses, and tinted lenses. The only documented exception is for urgent medical necessity, which requires a supporting doctor's note submitted alongside the application. Contact lenses that do not alter the natural eye colour are permitted.

Head Coverings

Head coverings of any kind are not permitted unless required for religious or medical reasons. Where permitted, the head covering must not create shadows on the face or obscure any part of the forehead, cheeks, chin, or ears.

Recency

The photo must have been taken within the last six months and must accurately reflect the applicant's current appearance. Significant changes in appearance — weight change, different hair colour, or facial hair that was not present in the photo — can lead to delays or requests for a new photo during the PSB appointment.

Avoid Rejection at the PSB Counter

PSB appointments for passport applications in China can take days or weeks to reschedule. A rejected photo means starting the entire process again. Our tool guarantees the precise 33×48mm dimensions, the 58–69% head height, pure white background, and 300 DPI print quality — the four most common failure points — before you ever reach the counter.

Digital vs Physical Submission

Chinese passport applications submitted in person at a PSB office require a physical photo to be pasted directly onto the application form. The photo must be printed on glossy finish photo paper — matte, inkjet, and plain paper prints are rejected. The photo surface must be clean, undamaged, and free of creases, perforations, or pen marks.

Some online pre-registration portals used by overseas Chinese applicants also require a digital upload. Our service delivers both: a high-resolution 826×1200 pixel JPEG for digital submissions, and a printable A4 template with multiple 33×48mm copies laid out for printing at any photo lab, pharmacy print counter, or home printer loaded with glossy photo paper.

Frequently Asked Questions — Chinese Passport Photo

Can I use a 35×45mm European photo for a Chinese passport?

No. The required size is 33×48mm. A 35×45mm photo is 2mm too wide and 3mm too short, and will be rejected at the PSB counter. You need a photo cropped specifically to 33×48mm with the correct head height ratio.

Does the background have to be pure white or can it be light grey?

Pure white only for Chinese passport photos. Light grey backgrounds, which are accepted in many European countries, do not meet MPS requirements. Our background removal tool applies a verified pure white (#FFFFFF) replacement.

My PSB office is in a different city — can I get the photo done online?

Yes. Our service produces a print-ready A4 template you can have printed at any photo lab or pharmacy before travelling to your PSB appointment. The digital JPEG is also available for any online pre-registration portals.

These requirements have been in official effect since 19 December 2016 and remain fully current as of 2026. The required photo size is 33mm wide × 48mm high, identical to the Chinese passport photo size, and different from every other major visa format in the world.

Since the China Online Visa Application (COVA) system launched in late 2025, every application begins with a digital photo upload that is checked algorithmically before a consular officer sees the form. COVA Digital Upload — What the Portal Checks in 2026

The COVA portal runs four automated checks the moment you upload a photo. All four must pass before your application can proceed:

  • File format: JPEG only. PNG, HEIC, TIFF, and WebP are all rejected outright.
  • Pixel dimensions: Width must be 354–420 pixels; height must be 472–560 pixels. Files outside this range — including photos resized to exactly 33×48mm at a different DPI — are rejected.
  • File size: The JPEG must be between 40 KB and 120 KB. A file of 121 KB fails. A file of 39 KB fails. There is no tolerance on either boundary.
  • Background colour: The portal enforces pure white. Off-white walls, cream studio backgrounds, and light grey backdrops all generate upload errors. This is stricter than most countries, which accept light grey or light blue.

Our output is automatically compressed and validated to land within the 40–120 KB range, within the accepted pixel dimensions, and with a verified white background — all in a single JPEG file.

Important: Digital Upload Does Not Replace the Printed Photo

Even after a successful COVA digital upload, you must bring one printed 33×48mm photo on glossy paper to your CVASC (Chinese Visa Application Service Center) appointment. The digital upload is not a substitute for the physical photo. Our A4 print template is included in your download for exactly this purpose.

Complete China Visa Photo Requirements — Checklist

Photo Size and Format

Print size: 33×48mm. This is not the same as the European 35×45mm standard or the US 2×2 inch (51×51mm) format. A US passport photo submitted for a Chinese visa is one of the most common rejection reasons at CVASC centres globally. Digital file: JPEG, 354–420 × 472–560 pixels, 40–120 KB.

Background

Pure white (#FFFFFF) with no shadows, gradients, patterns, or colour cast. Unlike many countries that accept light grey or light blue backgrounds for visa photos, Chinese consular requirements specify white only. The COVA portal rejects any background with a detectable colour shift away from pure white.

Facial Expression and Head Position

A neutral expression with eyes fully open and mouth closed. Both eyes must look directly at the camera. The head must be centred in the frame, with the face occupying 58–69% of the image height (chin to crown). A head tilt of up to 20° left or right and up to 25° up or down is technically permitted by the official MFA guidelines, though a straight-on angle always produces the cleanest biometric result and the lowest rejection risk.

Ears

Both ears must be fully visible. This is a specific Chinese consular requirement that differs from many Western passport standards. Hair covering the ears must be pinned back before the photo is taken. Our compliance checker flags obscured ears and prompts a retake before you reach the download step.

Eyeglasses

Plain prescription glasses without thick frames, tinting, or reflective glare are currently permitted for Chinese visa photos — this differs from Chinese passport photos, where glasses are prohibited entirely. However, any glasses that produce glare, shadow on the face, or reflections across the lenses will be rejected. The safest approach is to remove glasses for the photo.

Clothing

Casual clothing that contrasts with the white background is acceptable. White or very light-coloured tops must be avoided — they merge with the background and cause the border-detection algorithm to fail. Uniforms and clothing resembling military attire are prohibited. There is no requirement for formal or business dress.

Head Coverings

Head coverings are only permitted for documented religious reasons. Where worn, the covering must not create shadows on the face or obscure any part of the forehead, cheeks, chin, or ears. A secular hat, cap, or beret is not permitted under any circumstance.

Print Quality

The physical photo submitted at the CVASC appointment must be printed on glossy finish photo paper. Matte paper, plain paper, and standard inkjet paper are rejected. The printed photo must be undamaged — no creases, perforations, pen marks, or adhesive residue on the front surface.

Recency

The photo must have been taken within the last six months and must reflect the applicant's current appearance. This requirement is stated in the official MFA guidance effective 19 December 2016 and has not been amended.

China Visa Photo — Children and Infants

Every child requires their own individual visa and their own individual photo. The same 33×48mm size, white background, and 58–69% head height requirements apply to children's visa photos. The child must be photographed alone — no parent, guardian, or caregiver may appear in the frame. Infants under 6 months are granted one relaxation: their eyes may be closed. A white or near-white blanket or infant seat provides a suitable background for infant photos. Our tool includes a dedicated infant mode that adjusts the head-height calculation for smaller face proportions.

Common Rejection Reasons and How Our Tool Fixes Them

  • Wrong dimensions (35×45mm or 2×2 inch submitted instead of 33×48mm): Our tool re-crops to exactly 33×48mm with facial landmark positioning to maintain the correct head ratio.
  • Background not pure white: AI background removal replaces any original background with verified pure white (#FFFFFF) that passes COVA portal checks.
  • File outside 40–120 KB or wrong pixel dimensions: Output is automatically sized and compressed to land within the COVA-accepted range.
  • PNG format submitted instead of JPEG: Output is always saved as JPEG — the only format COVA accepts.
  • Ears obscured by hair: Compliance checker detects and flags covered ears before download.
  • Head size outside 58–69%: Facial landmark detection measures chin-to-crown height and re-crops to the compliant zone automatically.

One Download. Both Submissions Covered.

Your $3.99 download includes the COVA-ready digital JPEG (354–420 × 472–560 px, 40–120 KB) for your online application and a printable A4 template with multiple 33×48mm copies for your CVASC appointment. Both files are generated from the same validated, compliance-checked source image — ensuring your digital upload and your physical photo are identical, as consular staff require.

Frequently Asked Questions — China Visa Photo

Is the China visa photo the same size as the Chinese passport photo?

The print dimensions are the same — 33×48mm. However, eyeglasses are permitted (with conditions) in visa photos but prohibited entirely in passport photos. The digital file specifications also differ: visa photos submitted via COVA must be 354–420 × 472–560 pixels and 40–120 KB, while the passport application uses a physical-only submission at most PSB offices.

Can I use a photo taken on my smartphone?

Yes, provided the result meets all specifications. Take the photo in good natural light facing a window, at eye level, at least 1.5 metres from the camera, with a plain white or near-white wall behind you. Upload the file to our tool — we handle the background removal, cropping, sizing, and JPEG compression automatically.

Why does the COVA portal reject my photo even though it looks correct?

The most common invisible causes are: file size just outside the 40–120 KB band, pixel dimensions that are technically correct at one DPI but fall outside the 354–420 × 472–560 pixel range at another, or a background that appears white on screen but has a slight colour cast the portal detects. Passeport Biométrique Français : Normes Photographiques Officielles 2026

La demande et la délivrance d'un passeport biométrique français sont des procédures hautement sécurisées supervisées par le Ministère de l'Intérieur et gérées par l'Agence Nationale des Titres Sécurisés (ANTS). Contrairement à une idée reçue très répandue, la photographie d'identité pour un passeport français ne se conforme pas au standard international du fond blanc. La France maintient une exigence spécifique et non négociable : la photo doit impérativement mesurer 35mm de largeur sur 45mm de hauteur et être réalisée sur un fond bleu clair uni (Gris Bleu Clair - Référence Pantone 290C). Cette particularité chromatique est une source majeure d'erreurs et de refus lors du dépôt des dossiers en mairie ou dans les consulats. La norme est strictement encadrée par l'arrêté du 5 février 2009 relatif aux titres sécurisés, modifié par le décret du 18 novembre 2016. En plus de la couleur du fond, le cadrage du visage doit répondre à des critères biométriques très précis : la hauteur du visage, mesurée de la pointe du menton au sommet du crâne (hors chevelure), doit représenter entre 71% et 80% de la hauteur totale du cliché de 45mm. Cela correspond à un visage mesurant entre 32mm et 36mm sur la photo imprimée. Un visage trop petit ou trop grand entraîne le rejet automatique du fichier par le système de vérification en ligne de l'ANTS. Notre outil est spécifiquement calibré pour respecter cette fourchette de tolérance et générer le fond bleu clair homologué par l'administration française.

Spécifications Techniques de la Photo Française

Dimensions
35x45 mm
Fond
Bleu Clair (Light Blue)
Hauteur du Visage
71% - 80%
Fichier Numérique
413x531 px @ 300 DPI

Pourquoi le Fond Bleu Clair est-il Obligatoire en France ?

La persistance du fond bleu clair pour les titres d'identité français n'est pas une simple tradition administrative ; elle répond à une logique technique et biométrique précise. Le choix du bleu clair (plutôt que le blanc standardisé par l'OACI) a été validé par les ingénieurs de l'ANTS pour deux raisons principales :

  • Contraste et Détection des Contours : Le fond blanc pose un problème significatif pour la reconnaissance faciale automatisée lorsque l'utilisateur a les cheveux blancs, gris ou très blonds. Dans ces cas, la chevelure se fond dans l'arrière-plan, rendant la détection du contour crânien extrêmement difficile pour l'algorithme. Le fond bleu clair crée un contraste suffisant avec toutes les couleurs de peau et de cheveux, permettant au logiciel de l'ANTS d'isoler avec précision l'ovale du visage pour le stockage de l'empreinte biométrique dans la puce électronique du passeport.
  • Lutte Contre la Fraude Documentaire : La teinte exacte du fond (proche du Pantone 290C) est difficile à reproduire parfaitement avec un logiciel de retouche amateur. Les tentatives de modification d'un fond blanc en fond bleu avec des outils grand public laissent souvent des artefacts numériques ou une teinte trop cyan ou trop grise. Le système de contrôle automatisé de l'ANTS analyse la signature colorimétrique du fichier. Si le code hexadécimal ou la balance des couleurs ne correspond pas au nuancier officiel de l'Imprimerie Nationale, la photo est rejetée au moment du téléversement en ligne. Notre IA remplace l'arrière-plan par la nuance de bleu clair réglementaire exacte, garantissant l'acceptation par les serveurs de l'ANTS.

Liste de Conformité pour le Passeport Français : Vérifications en Mairie

Le dépôt du dossier de passeport s'effectue en personne dans une mairie équipée d'une station biométrique. L'agent d'état civil est formé pour vérifier la conformité de la photo d'identité à l'aide d'une planche de contrôle et d'un logiciel d'aide à la vérification. Pour éviter un refus qui vous obligerait à reprendre un rendez-vous parfois plusieurs semaines plus tard, respectez scrupuleusement la liste suivante :

  • Expression du Visage : L'expression doit être parfaitement neutre. La bouche doit être fermée et les lèvres jointes. Il est interdit de sourire, même légèrement, car cela modifie la position des commissures des lèvres et fausse la biométrie. Le regard doit être dirigé droit vers l'objectif, les yeux bien ouverts et visibles. Le visage doit être entièrement dégagé : aucune mèche de cheveux ne doit couvrir les yeux ou les sourcils.
  • Luminosité et Ombres : L'éclairage doit être parfaitement homogène et diffus. Les ombres portées sont strictement interdites. Cela inclut les ombres sur le fond bleu derrière la tête, les ombres sous le menton, et les ombres projetées par les cheveux sur le visage. Une photo présentant un côté du visage plus sombre que l'autre sera refusée pour "éclairage non conforme".
  • Tenue Vestimentaire et Accessoires : Il est formellement interdit de porter des vêtements bleu clair ou blancs qui se confondraient avec le fond. Privilégiez les hauts de couleur foncée (noir, marine, gris anthracite, bordeaux) pour créer un contraste net au niveau des épaules. Le port de lunettes est strictement interdit, même s'il s'agit de verres correcteurs sans monture apparente. Les reflets et les déformations optiques gênent la capture de l'iris. Les couvre-chefs ne sont tolérés que pour des raisons médicales ou religieuses attestées, et à condition que l'ovale du visage, du menton au front, soit totalement visible et dégagé.
  • Qualité d'Impression : Si vous fournissez une photo papier, elle doit être imprimée sur du papier photo mat de haute qualité. Le papier brillant est interdit car il provoque des reflets parasites lors du scan par le dispositif de recueil biométrique en mairie. La photo ne doit comporter ni pliure, ni agrafe, ni trace de colle.

Pourquoi Choisir Notre Outil pour la France ?

Les cabines Photomaton agréées ANTS sont une solution fiable, mais elles ne sont pas toujours accessibles, notamment pour les Français établis à l'étranger ou dans les zones rurales. De plus, une photo prise chez un photographe non spécialisé dans les normes ANTS a de très fortes chances d'être refusée en raison du fond blanc. Notre outil vous permet de générer une e-photo numérique parfaitement conforme aux exigences de l'administration française. Le fichier est calibré au pixel près (413x531) avec la teinte de fond homologuée et un cadrage du visage situé précisément dans la fourchette des 71-80%. Cela vous assure une tranquillité d'esprit totale lors de votre rendez-vous en mairie.

Soumission Numérique et le Système du "Code Photo"

Le système français repose sur un mécanisme unique en Europe : le Code Photo. Lorsque vous effectuez une pré-demande de passeport en ligne sur le site de l'ANTS, vous avez deux options : saisir manuellement un code à 22 caractères fourni par un photographe agréé (cabine Photomaton), ou téléverser directement un fichier photo numérique. Notre outil génère un fichier JPEG au format exact exigé par le portail de téléversement de l'ANTS. Une fois la photo validée par le système automatisé, la pré-demande est acceptée et vous pouvez vous rendre en mairie pour la finalisation. Notez que même si vous utilisez une photo numérique, il est fortement recommandé d'apporter un tirage papier de cette même photo le jour du rendez-vous, car certaines mairies préfèrent scanner une photo papier plutôt que de se fier uniquement au fichier numérique transmis. Notre planche d'impression au format A4 vous permet d'imprimer facilement et précisément une photo 35x45mm conforme, avec un visage mesurant exactement entre 32 et 36 mm de hauteur.

France Schengen Visa Photo Requirements: The Essential Guide for 2026

Applying for a visa to enter France—whether it is a short-stay Schengen Visa (Type C) or a long-stay National Visa (Type D)—requires strict adherence to the photographic standards established by the French Ministry of Foreign Affairs and the Agence Nationale des Titres Sécurisés (ANTS). Unlike many other Schengen member states that have transitioned to accepting standard ICAO white-background photographs, French consulates worldwide maintain a distinctive and rigorously enforced requirement: the photo must measure 35mm in width by 45mm in height and must be taken against a plain light blue background (Gris Bleu Clair). The submission of a photograph with a white or off-white background is the single most common reason for the rejection of French visa applications at the initial document screening stage. This is not a minor bureaucratic detail; the French visa system, integrated with the central EU Visa Information System (VIS), is calibrated specifically for the contrast provided by the light blue backdrop. When a visa officer scans a photograph, the biometric software performs a facial contour extraction. The light blue background ensures that the subject's hair and jawline are clearly delineated, regardless of whether the applicant has blonde, grey, or white hair. A white background often causes the software to clip or blur the edges of the face, resulting in a biometric template that is incomplete or invalid. To ensure your visa application proceeds without administrative delay, it is imperative to understand the specific technical and biometric parameters of the French visa photograph.

France Visa Biometric Targets & Technical Specifications

Physical Size
35mm x 45mm
Background Color
Light Blue (Pantone 290C)
Head Height Range
71% - 80% (32mm-36mm)
Digital File Specs
JPEG, 413x531 px

VIS Integration and Consular Biometric Capture

The photograph you submit with your French visa application is not merely affixed to the visa vignette; it is digitized and permanently stored in the Visa Information System (VIS). This centralized European database allows border guards at any Schengen entry point (including Paris Charles de Gaulle, Nice, or Lyon airports) to verify your identity against the photo on file. The capture process at the consulate is exacting. The consular scanner is programmed to recognize and reject images that do not meet the following technical parameters:

  • Facial Recognition Algorithm Compatibility: The French border police (Police aux Frontières - PAF) use advanced facial recognition gates (PARAFE). These gates compare the live image of the traveler with the digital photo stored in the VIS or on the passport chip. For this comparison to succeed, the original visa photo must have the head size correctly proportioned. The French system requires the head to occupy 71% to 80% of the vertical frame. If the head is too small (e.g., 60%), the system cannot extract enough pixel data from the iris and facial features for a positive match. If the head is too large (e.g., 85%), the reference points around the jaw and crown are missing, leading to a biometric mismatch and manual referral to a border officer.
  • Background Color Rejection Algorithm: The France-Visas online portal and the consular software perform an automated color histogram analysis. If the background is detected as white (RGB values near 255, 255, 255) instead of the expected light blue (approx. 215, 225, 240), the upload will fail or the application will be flagged for manual review. Our service replaces the background with the exact shade of light blue required by the French Ministry of Foreign Affairs, ensuring seamless integration with the VIS database.
  • Facial Feature Visibility: The French consular guidelines explicitly prohibit the wearing of glasses in visa photographs. This is a strict interpretation of the ICAO recommendation aimed at preventing iris obstruction. Tinted lenses, thick frames, or even light reflections on anti-reflective coating are grounds for photo rejection.

Key Compliance Checklist for French Visa Photographs

To avoid the issuance of an "Exigence de photographie conforme" (Request for Compliant Photo) notice, which effectively pauses your visa application processing time, ensure your photo meets the following checklist items derived from the official French consular guide:

  • Facial Expression and Pose: The expression must be completely neutral. The mouth must be closed with no visible teeth. A smile, even a slight one, will be rejected. The face must be centered horizontally and the head must not be tilted. The eyes must be open and looking directly at the camera lens. The entire face from chin to crown must be visible and unobstructed by hair.
  • Lighting and Shadow: The lighting must be diffused and even. Shadows on the face, particularly under the eyes, nose, or chin, are not tolerated. There should be no shadow cast on the light blue background. The photo should not be overexposed (washed out) or underexposed (too dark).
  • Attire and Accessories: Do not wear white or light blue clothing. These colors will blend into the background, causing the biometric software to miscalculate the outline of your shoulders and neck. Dark-colored clothing (navy, black, charcoal) is strongly recommended to provide clear contrast. Head coverings are only permitted for proven religious or medical reasons, and a signed declaration must accompany the application. Even then, the full facial oval must be visible.
  • Photo Quality and Recency: The photo must be recent (taken within the last six months) and accurately reflect your current appearance. Significant weight change, beard growth, or new hairstyles relative to the photo can lead to questioning at the border. The print must be on high-quality matte or semi-matte paper; glossy paper reflects light during scanning and renders the image unusable.

Ensuring a Smooth Visa Application Process

The process of gathering the required documentation for a French visa—including flight itineraries, hotel bookings, travel insurance, and financial statements—is already time-consuming. A photo rejection adds unnecessary stress and delay. In many French consulates, particularly in high-volume jurisdictions, a rejection for a non-compliant photo means the application is returned to the applicant, and the appointment slot is forfeited. The applicant must then schedule a new appointment, potentially waiting weeks for availability. Our AI photo tool mitigates this risk entirely. By generating a 35x45mm file with the precise light blue background and the face scaled to the 71-80% range, we provide a photo that meets the stringent technical requirements of the French consular network and the VIS database. This allows you to submit your visa application with confidence, knowing that the biometric component of your file is correct, allowing consular staff to focus on the substantive elements of your travel authorization.

Digital Upload for France-Visas Portal

Most French visa applications are now initiated online via the official France-Visas portal. During the online process, you will be prompted to upload a digital photograph. The portal performs an automated validation check on this file. The specifications for this digital upload are precise: the file must be a JPEG, and the dimensions must be exactly 413 pixels wide by 531 pixels high. This corresponds exactly to the 35x45mm print size at a resolution of 300 DPI. Uploading a photo with different dimensions (e.g., 600x800) will cause the portal to either stretch or compress the image, distorting the facial proportions and causing the head to fall outside the 71-80% requirement. Our download package includes the exact 413x531 JPEG file required for the France-Visas portal. The file is optimized for size and color profile (sRGB) to ensure a smooth upload without error messages. By using a photo formatted specifically for the French visa system, you ensure that the first step of your application—the biometric validation—is completed successfully and instantly.

Germany Passport Photo Requirements 2026: Complete Biometric Guide (Passbild Anforderungen)

Germany enforces some of the strictest biometric passport photo standards in Europe. Requirements are defined by the Federal Ministry of the Interior (Bundesministerium des Innern, BMI), the Bundesdruckerei GmbH, and aligned with ICAO Document 9303. Whether you are applying for a new Reisepass or renewing one at your local Bürgeramt, your photo must satisfy every technical parameter. A non-compliant image causes immediate rejection — and given long appointment wait times, this can delay your travel plans by weeks.

🆕 2025 Update: Digital-Only Biometric Photo Submission

The most important change for 2026 is the mandatory shift to digital photo submission. As of 1 May 2025, printed paper passport photos are no longer accepted for German passport, national ID card (Personalausweis), and electronic residence permit (eAT) applications inside Germany. You now have two compliant paths: have your german biometric photo taken at a Bürgeramt self-service terminal (typically €6), or visit a certified photo studio authorised to upload your image to the official Bundesdruckerei cloud platform. The studio provides a data matrix code (QR code) valid for six months, which the authority scans at your appointment. Self-taken photos, standard photo booth prints, and uncertified digital files are not accepted for domestic applications. If you are applying at a German consulate abroad, the traditional 35×45 mm printed or digital photo is still required — the digital-only rule applies only to domestic ID document applications inside Germany.

Official Technical Specifications — German Biometric Passport Photo 2026

Photo Size
35 × 45 mm
Background
Light Grey (Not White)
Head Height
32–36 mm (70–80%)
Digital Resolution
413 × 531 px @ 300 dpi

Biometric Passport Photo Size Germany: Core Specifications

The biometric passport photo size for Germany is exactly 35 mm wide and 45 mm tall. The face — measured from chin to the top of the head including hair — must occupy between 32 mm and 36 mm, which equals 70–80% of the total image height. This is a firm requirement in the Bundesdruckerei's Lichtbild-Mustertafel. The head must be perfectly centred, upright, and photographed directly from the front. No tilt, rotation, or angle is permitted. The background must be a plain, uniform light grey — pure white is rejected. Germany is unique among major EU passport-issuing countries in enforcing this grey background requirement. The reason is technical: Bundesdruckerei uses laser gravure to embed the photo into the passport's polycarbonate layer, and a white background causes overexposure artefacts that degrade the biometric template. For applicants with light or grey hair, a slightly darker shade of grey is recommended to maintain visible contrast.

Full Compliance Checklist for Your German Passport Photo

  • Expression: Strictly neutral. Mouth closed, no smiling — even subtle movement of mouth muscles alters facial geometry and triggers rejection by biometric verification software.
  • Eyes: Fully open, looking directly into the camera. Both irises must be completely visible. Gaze must not be directed sideways or downward.
  • Glasses: Generally prohibited. Even anti-reflective lenses can produce micro-glare that obscures the iris. A verified medical exemption is the only exception — with zero reflections and no tinted lenses.
  • Lighting: Even and shadow-free across the face and background. No shadows under the chin, in eye sockets, or behind the ears.
  • Head coverings: Only permitted for documented religious reasons. The full facial oval — chin to forehead, ear to ear — must remain visible.
  • Clothing: Dark or coloured clothing recommended. Avoid white or very light tops that can visually merge with the grey background.
  • Recency: The photo must reflect your current appearance and be taken within the past six months.
  • Image quality: Sharp, full colour, no pixelation, blurring, red-eye, or manipulation to facial features.

Get a Compliant German Biometric Passport Photo in Minutes

Our AI tool is trained on the exact 2026 BMI and Bundesdruckerei specifications. Upload a clear selfie and we automatically apply the correct light grey background, scale your face to the required 32–36 mm head height, and produce a print-ready 413×531 px JPEG at 300 dpi. The result is ready for a certified studio cloud upload or for direct use in a German consulate visa application abroad. Get the correct biometric photo size for Germany — right the first time.

Sources

Requirements verified against: Bundesportal (verwaltung.bund.de); BMI — Act to Strengthen Security in Passport Documents (2020, effective May 2025); Bundesdruckerei GmbH — Lichtbild-Mustertafel; ICAO Document 9303 Part 9; ISO/IEC 19794-5.

Germany Visa Photo Requirements 2026: Complete Guide for Schengen & National Visa Applicants

Applying for a Germany visa — whether a short-stay Schengen Visa (Category C) for tourism or business, or a long-stay National Visa (Category D) for work, study, or family reunification — requires a biometric photograph that meets the strict standards of the German Federal Foreign Office (Auswärtiges Amt) and the Federal Ministry of the Interior (BMI). These visa photo requirements for Germany follow ICAO Document 9303 and EU Visa Code Regulation (EC) No 810/2009, and are among the most rigorously enforced in the Schengen Area. Submitting a non-compliant visa photo for Germany means your application will not be accepted at the application centre, or will be rejected at the consulate — forcing a costly second appointment.

Important: The Digital-Only Rule Does Not Apply to Visa Applicants Abroad

Germany's domestic mandate for digital-only photos (effective May 2025) applies only to applications for German passports, ID cards, and residence permits submitted inside Germany. If you are applying for a Germany visa photo at a German embassy or consulate abroad — or through VFS Global or TLScontact — you are still required to submit a standard 35×45 mm printed biometric photograph. Two identical copies are typically required.

Germany Visa Photo — Official Technical Specifications 2026

Physical Size
35 × 45 mm
Background
Light Grey (Neutral)
Head Height
32–36 mm (70–80%)
Digital File (VIDEX)
JPEG, 413 × 531 px

Photo Size for Germany Visa: Exact Specifications

The photo size for Germany visa applications is 35 mm wide × 45 mm tall. This applies to both Schengen and National Visa applications at German missions worldwide. For digital submission via the VIDEX online portal, the photo specification for Germany visa requires a JPEG file at exactly 413 pixels wide by 531 pixels tall (300 dpi, sRGB colour profile). Any deviation in pixel dimensions causes the portal to crop or distort the image, invalidating the biometric head measurements. The face must occupy 70–80% of the total image height — between 32 mm and 36 mm from chin to top of head. These are the same biometric photo size Germany standards used for passport applications, ensuring consistency across all German identity documents.

Why Germany Requires a Light Grey Background — Not White

Most Schengen states accept a white background. Germany does not. The Schengen visa photo requirements for Germany specifically call for a neutral light grey background for three technical reasons:

  • VIS Scanner Calibration: German consulates use high-speed biometric scanners calibrated to a grey reference target. A white background causes automatic exposure compensation to darken the entire image, degrading the facial biometric template stored in the central Visa Information System (VIS).
  • EasyPASS Border Control: At Frankfurt (FRA) and Munich (MUC) airports, the Bundespolizei use EasyPASS facial recognition gates that compare live camera feeds against the VIS photo. The matching algorithm is optimised for the contrast profile of a light grey background — white backgrounds increase the risk of a false-negative match at the border.
  • Anti-Fraud Detection: A grey background is significantly harder to manipulate digitally. Attempts to swap a white background to grey in photo editing typically leave a visible halo around the hairline. Consular officers and the automated VIDEX system are trained to detect these artefacts.

Schengen Visa Photo Requirements Germany: Full Compliance Checklist

Based on requirements published by the Auswärtiges Amt and the German Visumhandbuch, your visa photo for Germany must meet all of the following:

  • Size: Exactly 35 × 45 mm (printed) or 413 × 531 px JPEG (VIDEX digital upload).
  • Recency: Taken within the last six months, reflecting your current appearance.
  • Colour: Full colour only. Black-and-white photos are not accepted.
  • Head position: Perfectly centred, full face to camera, no tilt or rotation. Full facial oval must be visible.
  • Expression: Neutral, mouth closed, no teeth visible. Smiling is not permitted.
  • Lighting: Even and shadow-free across the face and background.
  • Glasses: Generally prohibited — Germany is particularly strict. Medical exemptions require a signed physician's statement, with fully visible eyes and zero glare.
  • Head coverings: Accepted for religious reasons only. Face from chin to forehead must remain fully uncovered.
  • Clothing: Avoid white or very light tops — they reduce contrast against the grey background.
  • Print quality: High-quality matte or semi-matte photo paper. Glossy prints are rejected due to scanner glare.
  • Quantity: Two identical photos are typically required for German embassy visa photo requirements at missions abroad.

German Business Visa Photo Requirements

The german business visa photo requirements are identical to those for tourist Schengen visas — 35×45 mm, light grey background, neutral expression, no glasses. There is no separate photo standard for business applicants. The same biometric photo Germany visa specifications apply regardless of whether you are applying for a Category C short-stay business visa or a Category D long-stay work visa. Ensure your photo reflects your current professional appearance, particularly if your hairstyle or facial hair has changed since your last application.

Pass the VFS Biometric Check — First Time, Every Time

VFS Global and TLScontact run automated biometric pre-checks before your Germany visa application is forwarded to the consulate. A photo with the wrong background, incorrect head size, or uneven lighting will fail this check on the spot. Our AI tool applies the exact light grey background specified by the Auswärtiges Amt, scales your face to the required 32–36 mm head height, and outputs a VIDEX-ready 413×531 px JPEG. The result satisfies both the visa photo requirements Germany automated scan and the consulate's manual review — eliminating the most common reason for delayed or rejected applications.

Sources

Requirements verified against: Auswärtiges Amt (auswaertiges-amt.de); EU Visa Code Regulation (EC) No 810/2009; ICAO Document 9303 Part 9; VFS Global Germany photo guidelines; how-to-germany.com (updated May 2026).

Ghana Biometric Passport Photo: 50x50mm Square

The Ghana Passport Office under the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Regional Integration has adopted a 50x50mm square photograph for the biometric passport application. This is a large, high-detail format. The photo must be printed on high-quality photo paper and have a pure white background. The facial biometrics are critical: the head must occupy between 71% and 80% of the vertical height of the image. Due to the square shape (50x50mm), ensuring the face is not cut off at the top or bottom while maintaining this ratio is a common challenge for applicants.

Ghana Technical Specifications

Size

Head Range

The 50x50mm Biometric Challenge

The Ghanaian e-passport uses a chip that stores a high-resolution image of your face. The 50x50mm size provides ample data for the Facial Recognition System (FRS) used at Kotoka International Airport. However, the square frame requires precise vertical centering. If the head is below 71%, the facial features are too small for the scanner. If it's above 80%, the top of the hair or chin is likely cropped. Our AI ensures the face is perfectly anchored within the 71-80% safe zone in the 50x50mm square.

Key Compliance Checklist for Ghana Passport

  • Facial Expression: Neutral expression. Mouth closed, eyes open and looking directly at the lens.
  • Lighting: Even lighting across the face. No shadows. The white background must be pure #FFFFFF.
  • Clothing: Dark clothing is strongly recommended. White or light-coloured tops will blend into the required white background, causing an 'undefined outline' error at the Passport Application Centre (PAC).

Professional Results for Online Application

The online passport application portal requires a digital upload that matches the physical print. Our tool provides an 1181x1181 pixel image that meets the exact 50x50mm print requirement, ensuring consistency and compliance throughout the application process.

Digital vs Physical Submission

You must present a physical photo at the PAC for scanning. Our printable A4 template ensures you can print a high-quality 50x50mm photo that matches the digital file submitted online, avoiding discrepancies that can delay processing.

Indian Passport Photo Requirements (2026) — Official Passport Seva ICAO Guidelines

India's passport photo rules changed significantly on 1 September 2025. The Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) officially adopted the ICAO Doc 9303 biometric standard for all passport applications — replacing the older 2×2 inch (51×51 mm) square format that had been in use for decades. All Indian passport applications submitted from 1 September 2025 onwards, whether through a Passport Seva Kendra (PSK) inside India or through Indian embassies and consulates abroad, must comply with the new ICAO-aligned specifications. Applications submitted with the old square format are now rejected outright at the portal upload stage. This guide reflects the current requirements published by the Passport Seva scheme at passportindia.gov.in.

The September 2025 ICAO Change — What You Need to Know

From Square to Portrait: The New 35×45 mm Standard

Before September 2025, Indian passport applicants at embassies and consulates abroad submitted a 2×2 inch (51×51 mm) square photo — the same format used for Indian visas. This format has now been replaced by the internationally standard 35×45 mm portrait (rectangular) format, which is used by the UK, EU countries, Australia, and most Commonwealth nations. If you are renewing your Indian passport from outside India — through VFS Global or BLS International — you must now use the new 35×45 mm format. The old square format will be rejected. Note that OCI card applications continue to use the square format (200–900 pixels, under 200 KB) on the OCI Services Portal — the passport and OCI requirements are now different and you will need separate photos for each.

Passport Seva Portal: Exact Upload Requirements

For online applications through the GPSP 2.0 portal at mportal.passportindia.gov.in, you must upload a JPEG image at exactly 630 × 810 pixels, under 250 KB. The portal's automated system rejects files outside these limits before the application can progress — it does not flag the file for manual review. Smartphone photos from modern cameras are typically 3–8 MB and must be resized and compressed before upload. The portal also requires a separate digital signature image at 140 × 60 pixels, under 250 KB, in black ink on a white background. The Passport Seva team recommends uploading from a desktop computer, as several error codes on the new GPSP 2.0 portal are triggered specifically by mobile browser uploads.

Face Coverage: The 80–85% Rule

The updated ICAO-aligned Passport Seva guidelines specify that the face — measured from chin to crown — must occupy 80–85% of the photograph height. This is a tighter framing requirement than the 70–80% range used under the old square format, and tighter than most other ICAO countries. Photos taken at arm's length or with the subject too far from the camera typically result in too much background and a face that is too small in frame. This is now one of the leading causes of automated rejection on the Passport Seva portal.

Glasses: Banned Since September 2025

Glasses of all types — prescription, reading, and fashion — are no longer permitted in Indian passport photos as of the September 2025 ICAO enforcement. This applies to all applications, whether submitted domestically at a PSK or through an embassy abroad. The only exception is a documented medical condition that prevents removal, supported by a doctor's certificate submitted with the application. Vision impairment alone is not an accepted reason — contact lenses are a suitable alternative. Sunglasses and tinted lenses remain never acceptable under any circumstances.

Background, Lighting, and Print Quality

The background must be plain white with no shadows, patterns, or off-white tones. Even subtle shadows behind the ears or under the chin are flagged by the Passport Seva portal's automated system. Even, diffuse lighting is required — direct flash causes overexposure on the forehead and nose, which also triggers rejection. The photo must show natural skin tone with appropriate brightness and contrast. For physical prints submitted at a PSK or sent to VFS Global, professional continuous-tone printing is required. Polaroid photos and output from ordinary inkjet printers are explicitly not accepted by the Embassy of India guidelines.

Children and Infants

Children follow the same 35×45 mm ICAO specifications as adults. For infants, the child must face the camera with eyes open. A parent may hold the infant from behind, but hands must not be visible in the frame. A white sheet can be used as a background when photographing a baby lying flat. No chair backs, toys, or other people may be visible in the photo.

e-Visa Photo Rules Explained

Square Format: Different from the New Passport Standard

The India e-Visa photo uses a square (1:1) format — the height and width of the image must be equal. This is an important distinction to understand in 2026. While Indian passport applications now require the rectangular 35×45 mm ICAO portrait format (introduced in September 2025), the e-Visa portal continues to require a square photo with a minimum of 350×350 pixels and a maximum of 1000×1000 pixels, as a JPEG file between 10 KB and 1 MB. Do not use your new ICAO passport photo for your e-Visa application — the formats are different and the portal's automated check will reject a non-square image.

Glasses: Not Permitted on e-Visa Photos

The official indianvisaonline.gov.in instructions state clearly that the photo must be taken without spectacles. This applies to all e-Visa categories. Unlike Australian visa photos, where clear prescription glasses with no glare are permitted, India's e-Visa portal does not allow any form of eyewear. Remove all glasses — including prescription glasses, reading glasses, sunglasses, and tinted lenses — before the photo is taken. Photos submitted with glasses visible will be flagged by the automated compliance check.

Selfies Are Not Accepted

The Bureau of Immigration's official e-Visa instructions advise that the photo should be taken by another person, not as a selfie. Selfies taken with a front-facing smartphone camera introduce lens distortion and off-centre framing that causes the automated photo check to fail. Position yourself in front of a plain white wall, have someone else hold the camera or phone at eye level approximately 50 cm away, and take multiple shots to ensure the face is fully centred.

Background Requirements

The background must be plain white or plain light-coloured, with no patterns, shadows, or visible objects. There must be no shadows on the face or the background. Avoid wearing white or very light clothing, as this reduces the contrast between your shoulders and the background and may cause the automated check to flag the image. Both the face and the top of the shoulders should be clearly visible and distinguishable from the background.

Photo Must Be Unedited

The e-Visa portal requires an accurate, unedited likeness of the applicant. Digital retouching, beauty filters, skin-smoothing, background replacement, and AI-generated enhancements are all prohibited. Red-eye is not accepted. The image must show the applicant's natural skin tone with even, diffuse lighting — no hotspots on the forehead or nose, and no heavy shadows under the chin or beside the face.

Passport Page: A Separate Upload Requirement

Every e-Visa application also requires a separate upload of the scanned biographical data page of your passport. This is a PDF file between 10 KB and 300 KB. It is a distinct document from the face photo and is checked independently. Applications that upload the passport page in the photo field — or the photo in the passport field — are rejected immediately. Keep both files clearly labelled before you begin the application.

The Bureau of Immigration states that indianvisaonline.gov.in is the only official website for India e-Visa applications. If your uploaded photo does not pass the portal's automated compliance check, the portal will notify you by email and allow a corrected re-upload before a final decision is made on your application.

name: Italy Passport
passportcontent:
Italian Passport Photo Requirements 2026: The Complete Fototessera Guide

The Italian biometric passport — passaporto elettronico — is issued by the Ministero dell'Interno through the Polizia di Stato and processed at local questure (police headquarters) across Italy. The photo standard used is the European biometric fototessera format, which is also aligned with ICAO Doc 9303 international standards for machine-readable travel documents. In 2026, these requirements remain unchanged in their core specifications and apply equally to passport renewals, first-time applications, and the electronic identity card (Carta d'Identità Elettronica, CIE). Getting your fototessera right before your questura appointment is essential — appointment slots can take months to secure, and a rejected photo means starting the process again.

Official Photo Dimensions and Format

The Italian passport photo must be exactly 35 mm wide by 45 mm tall — the standard European biometric format, often referred to as 3.5 × 4.5 cm or fototessera. This is distinct from the US 2×2 inch format, and submitting the wrong size is one of the most common causes of rejection at the questura. The face, measured from the bottom of the chin to the crown of the head, must fall between 32 mm and 36 mm — covering approximately 70 to 80 percent of the photo height. At 300 DPI, this corresponds to a digital image of 413 × 531 pixels. The file format for digital submissions is JPEG; for digital workflows such as the CIE-Online portal managed by the Ministero dell'Interno, the file must be under 500 KB and at least 552 × 630 pixels.

Italian Passport — Technical Specifications 2026

Background Requirements

The official Italian Consulate General requirement — as stated on the esteri.it government portal — specifies a white background. White is the safest and universally accepted choice across all Italian questure and consulates. Some questure may accept a very light, uniform grey for improved contrast with fair skin, but this is not guaranteed across all offices. Patterned, coloured, or shaded backgrounds — including shadows falling on the background behind the head — will result in rejection. For best results, stand at least one metre in front of a plain white wall and use diffused, even lighting to prevent the shadow behind the head that is the most common cause of background rejection in Italy.

Print Quality and Paper Type

Italian authorities are strict about print quality. The Polizia di Stato requires that photos be printed on high-quality photographic paper — matte or semi-matte finish is standard and preferred; some questure have rejected photos printed on glossy paper when glare affects legibility. The photo must be sharp, correctly exposed, and free of pixelation, blur, or visible inkjet dots. Damaged, creased, folded, or ink-marked photos are rejected. Our digital file is calibrated for 300 DPI professional lab printing, ensuring the final print meets the Polizia di Stato's high standards.

Facial Expression, Head Position, and Gaze

The Italian fototessera requires a neutral expression with the mouth closed. Smiling, frowning, or any exaggerated expression is not permitted, as the ICAO biometric standard requires the face to be in its resting, everyday position for reliable facial recognition. The face must be fully forward-facing — no tilt, no rotation to either side. Both eyes must be open and clearly visible, looking directly into the camera. The face must be fully visible without any part obscured by hair, accessories, or shadows. The photo must accurately reflect your current appearance.

Glasses, Accessories, and Clothing

The Polizia di Stato now actively discourages glasses in passport photos to avoid glare and shadow issues that can cause biometric scanning failures. While this is not an absolute ban, removing glasses is the safest approach. Sunglasses and tinted lenses are never permitted under any circumstance. Hats and head coverings are generally not allowed; exceptions exist for documented religious or medical reasons, in which case the face must still be fully visible and an appropriate supporting statement may be required. Everyday clothing is recommended — avoid white or very light-coloured tops, which can blend into the white background, and avoid uniforms or costumes. Jewellery and facial piercings are permitted as long as they do not obscure any part of the face.

Photo Recency and Current Appearance

The photo must have been taken within the last six months and must accurately reflect your current appearance. Italian consulates are particularly strict about this rule — even if a photo is within the six-month window, a significant change in hairstyle, hair colour, or facial hair may prompt a request for a newer photo to ensure the document accurately identifies the holder.

Children and Infants

The same fototessera specifications apply to children and infants. Only the child should appear in the photo — no parent, carer, or other person may be visible. Baby equipment, toys, and car seats must not appear. For infants who cannot hold their heads independently, laying them on a plain white or off-white surface works well; ensure no shadows fall across the face. Infants' eyes do not need to be fully open, but all other rules apply.

Evita Code e Rifiuti alla Questura

Securing a passport appointment at an Italian questura can take several months in 2026. A rejected photo wastes that appointment entirely. Our AI tool automatically crops your face to the 32–36 mm biometric zone within the 35×45 mm fototessera frame, sets a clean white background, and delivers a 300 DPI JPEG ready for professional printing — meeting the standards of the Polizia di Stato and the Ministero dell'Interno on the first attempt.

Italian Schengen Visa Photo Requirements 2026

Italy is a member of the Schengen Area, and all Italian visa photo requirements are governed by the EU Visa Code (Regulation EC 810/2009) alongside ICAO Doc 9303 biometric standards. These rules apply to every Italian short-stay Schengen visa (Type C) and long-stay national visa (Type D) application, whether submitted at an Italian Consulate, a VFS Global visa application centre, or via the Italian online pre-application portal. As confirmed on the official Italian Consulate General website (esteri.it), the photo for a Schengen visa application must be a recent passport-style photograph measuring 3.5 cm × 4.5 cm with a white background and full front view. Non-compliant photos are rejected without exception at the application stage.

Official Photo Dimensions and Technical Specifications

The required photo size for an Italian Schengen visa is 35 mm wide by 45 mm tall — the universal Schengen format. The face, measured from the bottom of the chin to the top of the crown, must be between 32 mm and 36 mm, placing it within the 70 to 80 percent biometric zone of the photo height. This is the same specification used across all 29 Schengen member states and is non-negotiable. The US 2×2 inch format is a different shape and size, and will not be accepted at Italian consulates. For in-person applications, the photo must be printed in full colour on high-quality photographic paper. For online submissions, the file must be in JPEG format; most Italian consular portals specify a minimum resolution of 600 × 750 pixels and a recommended 300 DPI output.

Background Rules

The Italian Consulate General specifies a white background for Schengen visa photos — this is the officially stated requirement on the esteri.it government portal. White is the universally accepted background colour across all 29 Schengen states and eliminates any risk of rejection on background grounds. Some guidance materials reference light grey as an acceptable alternative for contrast purposes, but white remains the stated standard. The background must be completely uniform — no gradients, shadows, patterns, or visible objects behind the subject are permitted. A shadow falling behind the head from poor lighting is one of the most frequent rejection causes and is entirely avoidable with correct positioning and diffused lighting.

Expression, Head Position, and Gaze

All Schengen visa photos must comply with ICAO biometric positioning standards. The face must be fully forward-facing with no tilt or rotation to either side. The expression must be completely neutral with the mouth closed — even a slight smile can be grounds for rejection at Italian consulates, which are among the stricter Schengen posts in Europe. Both eyes must be open, fully visible, and looking directly into the camera. The face must not be obscured by hair, accessories, or shadows. There must be no red-eye effect.

Glasses and Accessories

Sunglasses and tinted lenses are never permitted in Schengen visa photos. Clear prescription glasses are technically not banned under Schengen rules, but Italy allows them only if there is no glare, no shadow, and no heavy frame obscuring the eyes. Given that glare and shadow from glasses are common causes of biometric rejection, removing glasses entirely is the most reliable approach. Hats and head coverings are not permitted except for documented religious or medical reasons, and even then the full face must remain clearly visible.

Clothing and Colour Contrast

Everyday clothing is required — uniforms, costumes, and official-looking attire must not be worn. Avoid white and very light-coloured clothing, as these can blend into the white background and reduce the contrast needed for biometric processing. Dark clothing provides the best contrast against a white background. Jewellery is permitted as long as it does not obstruct any part of the face.

Number of Prints and Submission

For in-person Schengen visa applications at Italian consulates or VFS Global centres, two identical printed photographs from the same session are required. The photos must be printed on professional photographic paper — unmarked on both sides, free of creases, tears, or damage, and unaltered by computer software or filters. Photos from different sessions, even if taken on the same day, are not considered identical and may be rejected.

Visa Information System (VIS) Compliance

Your Schengen visa photo is enrolled into the European Visa Information System (VIS), where it is retained for five years alongside fingerprint biometrics. The VIS runs automated facial recognition checks against each uploaded image, which is why sharpness, correct exposure, and accurate colour rendering are technically essential — not just cosmetic preferences. Blurry, dark, or incorrectly exposed photos fail VIS enrolment and result in application rejection. Our AI tool produces a correctly exposed, sharp JPEG output that passes automated biometric checks.

Photo Recency

The photo must have been taken within the last six months. Italian consulates are noted for being particularly strict on this rule — a photo within the six-month window that no longer reflects the applicant's current appearance (due to changes in hairstyle, colour, or facial hair) may be rejected and a newer photo requested.

Get Your Italian Visa Photo Right — First Time

A rejected Schengen visa photo delays your entire application and can affect your travel dates. Our AI tool produces a biometric-compliant 35×45 mm photo with a white background, correct 32–36 mm face height, and a 300 DPI JPEG — meeting the standards required by the Italian Consulate General (esteri.it) and the EU Visa Code Regulation EC 810/2009 from the very first attempt.

  • Face height: 32–36 mm chin to crown; 70–80% of photo height.
  • Background: White — uniform, no shadows, no patterns.
  • Expression: Fully neutral, mouth closed, no smile.
  • Eyes: Both open, no red-eye, looking directly into the camera.
  • Head position: Full front, no tilt or rotation.

  • Clothing: Everyday attire, avoid white tops, no uniforms or costumes.

name: New Zealand Passport
passportcontent:
New Zealand Passport Photo Requirements 2026 — Complete DIA Official Guide

Getting your New Zealand passport photo right in 2026 is more important than ever. The Department of Internal Affairs (DIA) uses advanced automated biometric facial recognition software to verify every photo submitted through the official passports.govt.nz online renewal portal — and even minor errors in background, head size, or expression will trigger an instant rejection. Whether you are renewing your NZ passport online, applying for the first time, or submitting from overseas at a New Zealand embassy or consulate, this complete guide covers every official requirement you need to know.

The standard New Zealand passport photo size for printed applications is 35mm wide × 45mm tall — the same international ICAO biometric format used by Australia, the UK, and most of Europe. For online digital submissions through the DIA passport renewal portal, your JPEG photo must be between 900 × 1200 pixels and 4500 × 6000 pixels, with a file size between 500KB and 10MB. The photo must be in portrait orientation (taller than wide) with a 3:4 aspect ratio. Scanned photos and photos taken with the front-facing selfie camera are not accepted — the DIA requires someone else to take your photo using a rear camera from approximately 1.5 metres away to maintain correct facial proportions and avoid lens distortion.

Background Color — The #1 Reason NZ Passport Photos Get Rejected

The background of your New Zealand passport photo must be plain, light-coloured, and completely uniform — with no patterns, textures, gradients, shadows, or objects visible anywhere in the frame. The DIA accepts white, light gray, cream, or light blue backgrounds, provided there is strong visual contrast between your face and the background. The background must be evenly lit — any shadow cast by your head or body onto the background is grounds for rejection. Stand at least 1–1.5 metres away from the background surface to prevent shadow formation. Do not digitally remove or replace your background using photo editing apps or AI tools — the DIA checker detects artificial backgrounds and flags them as non-compliant.

Head Size, Face Position, and Biometric Framing Requirements

Your head — measured from the bottom of your chin to the top of your hair — must occupy between 70% and 80% of the total photo height. In physical terms for a 35×45mm print, this means your head height must be between 32mm and 36mm. The face must be perfectly centered in the frame, with a clear visible gap between the top of your head and the top edge of the photo. Your full shoulders and upper chest must be included in the lower portion of the frame. The head must be held completely upright — no tilting, turning, or leaning in any direction. Both eyes must be at an equal height and positioned between 55% and 65% from the bottom of the photo frame, which is the standard ICAO biometric eye-line requirement used by the DIA's automated photo checker.

Facial Expression, Eyes, and Mouth Rules

The DIA requires a completely neutral facial expression for all New Zealand passport photos. Your mouth must be firmly closed — no smiling, no open mouth, no visible teeth. Your eyes must be fully open and looking directly into the camera lens. Squinting, half-open eyes, or eyes obscured by hair are all grounds for rejection. Your hair must be tucked away from your face, kept clear of your eyes and eyebrows, and must not cover the edges of your face. The photo must be a true, natural likeness — no filters, AI enhancement, skin smoothing, blemish removal, or any other form of digital retouching is permitted under DIA guidelines.

Glasses, Accessories, and Clothing Policy 2026

Prescription glasses are technically permitted for New Zealand passport photos if the lenses are completely clear and untinted, the frames do not obscure or cover the eyes, and there is zero glare or flash reflection on the lenses. However, the DIA strongly recommends removing glasses for the photo to eliminate any risk of rejection. Sunglasses, tinted glasses, thick-framed glasses, and colored contact lenses are strictly prohibited. Head coverings are not permitted unless worn daily for verified religious or medical reasons — in which case a signed statutory declaration is required with the application, and the complete face from forehead to chin must remain fully visible with no shadows. Clothing should be everyday attire in plain, contrasting colors — avoid white or very light clothing that may blend with a light background.

Children and Infant Passport Photo Rules

Every New Zealand child, including newborns, requires their own separate passport — and their photo must meet the same DIA standards as adults. For infants who cannot sit upright, lay the baby on a plain white or light-colored sheet and photograph from directly above. No parent hands, toys, pacifiers, or car seat structures should be visible in the frame. The baby's face must be fully visible and as close to neutral as possible — partially open eyes are tolerated for very young infants. For toddlers and older children, both eyes must be open, the mouth closed, and the face directed straight at the camera. The same 35×45mm dimensions and 70–80% head coverage rule apply regardless of age.
