New Zealand Visa Photo Requirements 2026 — Immigration NZ Official Standards

Planning to visit, work, or study in New Zealand? Whether you are applying for a New Zealand Visitor Visa, Work Visa, Student Visa, Skilled Migrant Visa, or the NZeTA (New Zealand Electronic Travel Authority), your photograph is one of the most carefully checked components of your application. In 2026, Immigration New Zealand (INZ) has tightened its digital photo verification process — automated biometric checks now reject non-compliant photos before a case officer ever reviews your file. Submitting a wrong-sized, incorrectly lit, or poorly framed photo is one of the most common and completely avoidable reasons visa applications are delayed or returned for resubmission.

The required New Zealand visa photo size is 35mm wide × 45mm tall for printed applications. For digital online submissions through the INZ visa portal or the NZeTA application system, the photo must be a JPEG file between 900 × 1200 pixels and 2250 × 3000 pixels, with a file size between 500KB and 3MB. For NZeTA applications specifically, the maximum file size is increased to 10MB. The image must be in portrait orientation (taller than wide, 3:4 ratio), in full color, taken with a rear-facing camera from approximately 1.5 metres away. Front-facing selfie camera photos are not accepted by the INZ portal.

Background Requirements for NZ Visa Photos

The background must be plain, light-coloured, and completely uniform — white, light gray, or cream are all acceptable, provided there is strong contrast between the background and your face, hair, and clothing. The background must contain no shadows, no patterns, no textures, no objects, and no other people. Lighting must be uniform across both your face and the background — uneven lighting that creates brighter or darker areas will cause rejection. Stand at least 1–1.5 metres from the background to prevent shadows. Do not use AI background removal tools or digital background replacement — Immigration NZ's automated photo checker flags artificially generated backgrounds as non-authentic submissions.

Face Size, Head Position, and Biometric Compliance

Your face must occupy 70% to 80% of the total photo height, measured from the bottom of your chin to the top of your head including hair. This is the standard ICAO biometric requirement applied consistently by Immigration New Zealand across all visa categories. The head must be perfectly centered in the frame, held upright with no tilt, rotation, or lean in any direction. Your full shoulders and upper chest must be visible at the bottom of the frame. Both eyes must be positioned between 55% and 65% from the bottom of the image — this eye-line positioning is verified automatically by the INZ digital photo checker. There must be a visible clear gap between the top of your head and the top edge of the photo.

Expression, Eyes, Lighting, and Photo Quality

Immigration New Zealand requires a completely neutral facial expression for all visa photos in 2026. The mouth must be firmly closed with no visible teeth. Your eyes must be fully open, looking directly into the camera. Hair must be tucked away from your face, clear of your eyes and eyebrows, and must not cover the sides of your face or the top of your forehead. The photo must be sharp, in precise focus, and free from motion blur, pixelation, red-eye, overexposure, or underexposure. Lighting must represent your true, natural skin tone — no flash hotspots or dark areas on the face are permitted. The photo must be a true, unmodified representation of your natural appearance — no beauty filters, skin-smoothing effects, blemish removal, or any digital alterations are permitted under INZ photo guidelines.

Glasses, Head Coverings, and Accessories for NZ Visa Photos

Prescription glasses are permitted for New Zealand visa photos provided they have completely clear, untinted lenses, zero glare or flash reflections, and do not cover or obstruct the eyes in any way. In practice, INZ and consular officers recommend removing glasses entirely to avoid the risk of rejection. Sunglasses, fashion glasses, thick-framed glasses, and tinted contact lenses are strictly prohibited. Head coverings are only permitted for verified religious or medical reasons — the full face including forehead, cheeks, and chin must be completely visible with no shadows cast. Jewelry and facial piercings are acceptable as long as they do not cover or obscure any facial features. Clothing should be plain everyday attire in mid-toned colors — avoid white or very light colors that may blend into a light background.

NZeTA Digital Photo Upload Requirements 2026

The NZeTA (New Zealand Electronic Travel Authority) — required for eligible visa-exempt nationalities traveling to New Zealand — requires a digital photo upload during the online application on the official NZeTA app or website. The photo must meet the same 35×45mm proportional ratio, 70–80% face coverage, light plain background, and neutral expression requirements as standard INZ visa photos. The file must be JPEG format, under 10MB, in portrait orientation. Citizens of the USA, UK, Canada, EU countries, and many others require NZeTA before travel — ensuring your digital photo is biometrically compliant is essential to avoid a failed NZeTA application.

name: Portugal Passport
passportcontent:
Portugal Passport Photo Requirements 2026 — IRN Official Guide (Passaporte Eletrónico Português)

The Passaporte Eletrónico Português (PEP) is issued by the Instituto dos Registos e do Notariado (IRN), the official Portuguese government authority responsible for all passport issuance across Portugal and through the Portuguese consular network worldwide. As an EU member state and part of the Schengen Area, Portugal's passport ranks among the most powerful travel documents in the world, granting visa-free or visa-on-arrival access to over 186 countries as of 2026. Meeting the IRN's biometric photo standards is essential — errors in photo format, head size, background, or expression are among the most common and easily avoided causes of application delays.

One of the most important facts to know before your appointment: for in-person passport applications at IRN offices in Portugal, you do not need to bring printed passport photos. Your biometric data — including your digital photograph, fingerprints, and signature — is captured directly at the IRN passport office during your appointment. However, if you are applying or renewing your passport through a Portuguese consulate abroad, two printed passport photos are typically required. In both cases, the photo must meet the official IRN biometric specifications described below.

Portugal Passport Photo Size — Official Dimensions

The required Portugal passport photo size is 35mm wide × 45mm tall (3.5 × 4.5 cm), the standard European ICAO biometric format. The digital file must be in JPEG format at a minimum of 300 DPI, equivalent to 413 × 531 pixels at print size. The head height — measured from the bottom of the chin to the top of the crown of the head, not the top of the hair — must be approximately 34.5mm, with a 3mm gap between the top of the head and the upper edge of the photo. This precise positioning requirement is strictly verified during IRN biometric pre-processing.

Background Color — White is the Official Portugal Passport Standard

Unlike some other Schengen countries that require a light gray background, the official Portugal passport photo background must be plain white. The background must be completely uniform — no gradients, no patterns, no visible textures, no shadows, and no other people or objects. Any shadow cast by the applicant's head or body onto the background surface is grounds for rejection. Adequate distance of at least one metre between the applicant and the background helps prevent shadow formation. The background must also provide strong visible contrast with the applicant's skin tone, hair, and clothing. A white or very light cream wall in good, even lighting is ideal.

Head Size, Face Position, and Biometric Centering

The face must be perfectly centered in the frame and must occupy between 70% and 80% of the total photo height. The head height must be approximately 34.5mm chin-to-crown, with a fixed 3mm top margin. The face must be fully visible and front-facing — no tilting, rotating, or leaning. Both sides of the face must be visible. Hair must not fall across the face, cover the eyebrows, or obscure the edges of the face. The shoulders and upper chest must be visible at the bottom of the frame. The camera must be held at the same horizontal level as the applicant's eyes.

Facial Expression and Eye Requirements

The IRN requires a completely neutral facial expression: mouth firmly closed, no smile, no frown, no raised eyebrows. Both eyes must be fully open and directed straight into the camera lens. Squinting, partially closed eyes, or eyes obscured by hair or accessories are not acceptable. The photo must be a true, natural representation of the applicant's appearance — no digital retouching, beauty filters, AI skin enhancement, or background manipulation is permitted. The IRN's processing system checks for image authenticity.

Glasses Policy 2026 — Not Permitted

Glasses are not permitted in Portuguese passport photos. This applies to prescription glasses, fashion eyewear, thick-framed glasses, sunglasses, and tinted lenses. This requirement surprises many applicants from countries such as Australia or the US where prescription glasses may be permitted under certain conditions — in Portugal, the rule is categorical. Remove all eyewear before your photo is taken. Colored contact lenses are also not allowed — the photo must reflect your natural eye color.

Clothing, Head Coverings, and Print Quality

Clothing should be everyday, plain-colored attire. Uniforms are not permitted. Avoid white or very light tops that may blend into the white background. Head coverings are not permitted unless worn daily for verified religious or medical reasons — in such cases, the full face from forehead to chin must be completely visible with no shadows. The IRN is particularly strict about print quality for consulate submissions: the printed photo must be sharp, in full color on high-quality photo paper, and completely free from creases, fingerprints, ink marks, or fading. Two identical printed photos are required for consular applications.

The background for a Portugal Schengen visa photo must be plain white or light gray, completely uniform with no patterns, textures, shadows, or objects. Dark backgrounds, colored backgrounds, cream, blue, or patterned backgrounds are all rejected. Lighting must be even across the background — any visible shadow cast by the applicant's head or body will result in rejection at VFS pre-check.

Face Size and Biometric Head Positioning

Your face must occupy 70% to 80% of the total photo height, measured from the bottom of the chin to the top of the crown (not the top of the hair). In millimeter terms, the head height must fall between 32mm and 36mm. The face width from ear to ear must be between 16mm and 20mm. Photos are automatically rejected if the face height falls below 27mm or exceeds 40mm. The face must be centered in the frame, held completely upright with no tilt, rotation, or lean. Both sides of the face must be clearly visible. The shoulders and upper chest must be visible at the bottom of the frame, with a clear gap between the top of the head and the top edge of the photo.

Expression, Lighting, and Photo Quality

The facial expression must be completely neutral: mouth firmly closed, no smile, no frown, no visible teeth, no raised or furrowed eyebrows. Both eyes must be fully open and directed straight at the camera. The photo must be sharp, in precise focus, in full color, and free from red-eye, motion blur, overexposure, underexposure, or pixelation. Lighting must be even across both the face and the background with no shadows on the face, neck, or background. The photo must be printed on high-quality matte or glossy photo paper, completely free of creases, fingerprints, ink marks, or damage. Digital retouching, beauty filters, AI skin enhancement, and background replacement are all strictly prohibited — VFS Global pre-check staff are trained to identify digitally altered photos.

For Portugal visa applications, Italy, Spain, and Portugal allow prescription glasses if there is absolutely no glare, no tinted lenses, and no obstruction of the eyes whatsoever — however, in practice, removing glasses entirely is strongly recommended to eliminate any rejection risk. Head coverings are permitted only for verified religious or medical reasons, with the complete face from forehead to chin fully visible and no shadows cast by the covering. Clothing should be plain, everyday attire in contrasting colors — avoid white or very light tops that may blend with a white or light gray background, and avoid uniforms of any kind.

All Portuguese long-stay visa categories — including the D7 Passive Income Visa, the D8 Digital Nomad Visa, the Golden Visa (ARI), and the D2 Entrepreneur Visa — use the identical 35×45mm, white or light gray background, 70–80% face coverage photo standard. Sweden Passport Photo Requirements 2026: The Complete Guide

Applying for a Swedish passport in 2026 means meeting some of the strictest biometric photo standards in Europe. Whether you are renewing an expiring document, applying for the very first time, or updating after a name change, understanding the exact technical requirements set by Polismyndigheten (the Swedish Police Authority) will save you from a costly, time-consuming rejection at the counter. Thousands of passport applications are delayed every year in Sweden because the submitted photo fails one or more of the biometric criteria — and the most common reason is that applicants simply did not know what the rules were. This guide covers every official requirement for a Sweden passport photo in 2026, including dimensions, background colour, face size, eye positioning, acceptable expressions, glasses rules, and the most frequent mistakes people make when taking a Swedish passport photo at home or in a photo booth.

Official Swedish Passport Photo Size and Dimensions

The correct Sweden passport photo size is 35 × 45 mm (width × height). At a resolution of 300 DPI this translates to exactly 413 × 531 pixels. These measurements are non-negotiable — submitting a photo that is even slightly the wrong size, whether printed or digital, will result in automatic rejection. Many applicants searching for the Swedish passport photo dimensions in inches should note that 35 mm equals approximately 1.38 inches and 45 mm equals approximately 1.77 inches. If you are printing at home or ordering online, always verify that your printing service delivers the photo at the correct physical size and at full 300 DPI resolution to ensure crisp, clear biometric detail.

Background Colour: Why Pure White Matters

The background of your Swedish passport photo must be plain white — not off-white, not light grey, not cream. Polismyndigheten's automated facial recognition systems are calibrated for a pure white background, and any colour cast, shadow, or gradient behind the head will cause the photo to be flagged or rejected. This is one of the most common reasons a Sweden passport photo taken in poor lighting or against a painted wall fails. Our AI background removal tool replaces any existing background with a perfectly calibrated white, ensuring your photo passes the automated background check every time. Avoid patterned walls, coloured curtains, and any background that creates contrast behind your shoulders or hair.

Head Size and Face Position: The 71–80% Rule

This is the requirement that trips up the most applicants. In a valid Swedish passport photo, your head must occupy between 71% and 80% of the total image height — meaning the distance from the top of your head (or hair) to the bottom of your chin must fall within that range relative to the full 45 mm photo height. That works out to roughly 32–36 mm of face height within the frame. If your head is too small — a very common result of standing too far from the camera — the photo will be rejected. If your head is too large and crops the top of your hair or forehead, it will also be rejected. Our AI automatically detects your facial landmarks and scales the crop to place your head at exactly the right size within the frame, every single time.

Eye Height Positioning in a Swedish Passport Photo

Beyond head size, Polismyndigheten also specifies where your eyes must appear vertically within the photo. Your eyes should be positioned between 56% and 69% from the bottom of the image — in other words, roughly in the upper-middle portion of the frame. This requirement exists to support automated biometric matching systems that use eye position as a primary reference point. When taking a Sweden passport photo at home, tilt the camera slightly downward if your eyes are appearing too high, or upward if they are sitting too low. Our AI crop engine handles this positioning automatically by detecting your eye coordinates and adjusting the frame accordingly.

Facial Expression: Neutral, Mouth Closed, Eyes Open

Swedish passport photo rules require a neutral facial expression with your mouth firmly closed and both eyes fully open. Smiling is not permitted — not even a slight, natural smile. This applies to adults and children alike. Your gaze must be directed straight at the camera lens, not upward, downward, or to the side. Blinking, squinting, or partially closed eyes will cause rejection. This neutral-expression rule exists because facial recognition algorithms used at border control perform significantly better when the geometry of the face is in a relaxed, symmetrical resting position. If you are taking a Sweden passport photo at home using a smartphone, take several shots and review them carefully before submitting — subtle micro-expressions are easy to miss on a small screen.

Glasses: Not Permitted in Swedish Passport Photos

Since 2015, glasses of any kind are not permitted in Swedish passport photos. This includes prescription glasses, reading glasses, sunglasses, tinted lenses, and contact lenses that alter eye colour. Even thin wire-frame glasses that appear transparent in photos must be removed. The reason is straightforward: frames and lenses can obscure parts of the eye area that biometric systems rely on, and lens reflections can interfere with iris recognition technology used at automated border crossings. If you normally wear glasses every day, simply remove them for the photo — you do not need a separate exemption or medical certificate to do so.

Lighting, Shadows, and Photo Quality

A technically valid Sweden passport photo must be sharp, in focus, and taken with even, diffuse lighting. There must be no shadows on the face or behind the head, no red-eye effect, and no motion blur. Natural daylight from a window is ideal for a home passport photo, but avoid direct sunlight that creates harsh shadows under the nose and chin. Flash photography should be used carefully — a direct frontal flash can wash out facial detail, while an off-axis flash creates unacceptable shadows. The photo must be in colour; black-and-white photos are not accepted. If you are submitting a digital photo for an online passport renewal or e-service application through Polismyndigheten's portal, the file must be a JPEG with a minimum resolution of 300 DPI and a file size that meets the portal's upload requirements.

Passport Photo for Children and Infants in Sweden

All Swedish citizens, including newborns, require their own individual passport. For infant and baby passport photos in Sweden, the same biometric rules apply — white background, neutral expression, eyes open, head within the 71–80% height range. For very young babies who cannot sit up independently, lay the infant on a white sheet and photograph from directly above, then crop the image to the correct 35 × 45 mm dimensions. Our AI can detect infant facial landmarks and adjust the crop accordingly, making it far easier to produce a valid baby passport photo in Sweden without visiting a professional studio.

Sweden Visa Photo Requirements 2026: Everything You Need to Know

If you are applying for a visa to enter Sweden in 2026 — whether as a tourist, student, worker, or family member — one of the most critical elements of your application is submitting a correctly formatted passport-style photograph. An incorrect visa photo is one of the leading causes of Schengen visa application delays and outright rejections at Swedish embassies, consulates, and Migration Agency offices worldwide. This comprehensive guide explains every official requirement for a Sweden visa photo in 2026, covering size, background, head positioning, expression, digital upload specifications, and the specific rules that apply when applying through Migrationsverket (the Swedish Migration Agency) or a Swedish diplomatic mission abroad.

Sweden Visa Photo Size: The 35 × 45 mm Standard

The required Sweden Schengen visa photo size is 35 × 45 mm — identical to the Swedish passport photo format. This is the standard biometric photograph format used across all 27 Schengen Area member states, established under EU Regulation 1683/95 and its subsequent amendments. At 300 DPI resolution, 35 × 45 mm corresponds to 413 × 531 pixels. Whether you are applying at the Swedish embassy in your home country, submitting through a VFS Global visa application centre, or uploading digitally through an online application portal, your photo must conform precisely to these dimensions. Photos that are too small, too large, or submitted at the wrong aspect ratio will be returned with a request to resubmit, delaying your application by days or even weeks.

Background Requirements for a Sweden Visa Photo

Your Sweden visa photo background must be plain white with no visible patterns, textures, or gradients. Unlike some other countries that accept off-white or very light grey backgrounds, Swedish consular guidelines and Schengen biometric standards specifically require pure white. There must be no shadows cast behind your head or on your shoulders. This is particularly important if you are taking a visa photo at home — even a slightly off-white wall, a bedsheet with subtle texture, or a poorly lit background can be grounds for rejection. Your face must be centred horizontally within the frame, and your eyes must fall within a vertical range of 56% to 69% from the bottom of the image. These measurements exist to support the automated facial recognition systems used by Swedish border authorities and the Schengen Information System (SIS). If your head is too small — a frequent error in DIY visa photos taken with the subject standing too far from the camera — the photo will fail the automated biometric check and your application will be returned.

Facial Expression and Gaze Direction

For a valid Sweden visa photo, your expression must be completely neutral. Your mouth must be closed, your lips relaxed, and your face in a natural resting position. Smiling — even a gentle, closed-mouth smile — is not acceptable under Schengen biometric photo rules. Both eyes must be fully open and looking directly at the camera. Your head must be held straight, facing forward, with no tilting, turning, or angling to either side. Wearing a headband, hair clip, or any accessory that obscures part of the face or hairline is not permitted unless it is worn for religious or medical reasons. In those cases, the face from forehead to chin and from ear to ear must remain fully visible.

Glasses Policy for Sweden Visa Photos in 2026

In line with updated ICAO biometric photograph standards adopted across the Schengen Area, glasses are not permitted in Sweden visa photos as of 2026. This applies to all types of eyewear including prescription glasses, reading glasses, sports glasses, and sunglasses. Even thin, nearly invisible frames must be removed before the photo is taken. The prohibition exists because frames can obscure the eye region and lens reflections — including anti-reflective coatings — can interfere with iris-scanning and facial geometry algorithms. If you wear glasses daily, you are not required to provide a note from an optician; simply remove them for the photograph.

Digital Photo Requirements for Online Visa Applications to Sweden

When applying for a Swedish visa online — including through the Migrationsverket self-service portal for residence and work permits, or through e-visa platforms at Swedish embassies — you will be required to upload a digital photograph rather than submit a printed one. The digital Sweden visa photo must be saved as a JPEG file at a minimum of 300 DPI. Most online systems also impose a maximum file size, typically between 2 MB and 5 MB. How Recent Must Your Sweden Visa Photo Be?

Your Sweden visa photo must have been taken within the last six months of your application date. This recency requirement exists because your appearance — particularly your facial structure, hairstyle, and weight — can change significantly over longer periods, which would reduce the reliability of biometric matching at border control. If you are applying for a long-stay visa or a residence permit that will be processed over several months, check with the Swedish embassy or Migrationsverket whether you need to resubmit a more recent photo before your permit is issued.

Common Reasons Sweden Visa Photos Are Rejected

Based on the most frequently reported issues at Swedish consulates and visa application centres worldwide, the most common reasons a Sweden visa photo is rejected in 2026 are: background that is not pure white; head too small (subject stood too far from camera); shadows on the face or behind the head; a visible smile or non-neutral expression; glasses that were not removed; a photo that is older than six months; incorrect printed dimensions; and poor image quality including blur, pixelation, or red-eye. Each of these issues is fully addressed by our AI photo tool, which validates your image against all of these criteria before generating your final download.

Swiss Passport (Schweizer Pass) Photo Requirements 2026

The Swiss passport, officially issued by the State Secretariat for Migration (SEM — Staatssekretariat für Migration) under the authority of the Swiss Federal Council, is one of Europe's most respected travel documents. As of 2026, it grants visa-free or visa-on-arrival access to over 186 countries, making it consistently ranked among the top five most powerful passports in the world. To maintain this status and support Switzerland's biometric border infrastructure, the Swiss government enforces precise and strictly verified photo standards that all applicants — whether applying in Switzerland or through a consulate abroad — must follow without exception.

The required photo size for all Swiss passport applications in 2026 is 35mm wide × 45mm tall (equivalent to 1.38 × 1.77 inches, or 3.5 × 4.5 cm). The digital resolution must be a minimum of 600 DPI, with a maximum digital file size of 240KB in JPEG format. For printed submissions — which remain mandatory for in-person consulate applications — the photo must be printed in full color on high-quality glossy or matte photo paper, free from scratches, creases, smudges, or fingerprints. Two printed photographs are typically required per application.

Background: A Critical 2026 Update — Light Gray, Not White

One of the most important distinctions for Swiss passport photos in 2026 is the background color requirement. Unlike many countries, Switzerland — as a Schengen Area member state — officially specifies a plain light gray or light blue background, not pure white. This aligns with the shared Schengen biometric photographic standard. The background must be completely uniform with no visible shadows, gradients, patterns, textures, or objects. Photographers or applicants using a white background risk automatic rejection. There must also be no shadows cast on the background by the applicant's body or head.

Head Size, Face Positioning, and Biometric Geometry

The face must be perfectly centered in the frame and occupy 70% to 80% of the total photo height. The head height — measured from the top of the skull (excluding hair) to the bottom of the chin — must fall between 32mm and 36mm. Crucially, the face width from ear to ear must measure between 16mm and 20mm. Photos will be automatically rejected if the face measures less than 27mm or more than 40mm in height. The head must be held completely upright — no tilting, rotating, or leaning to either side. The camera must be at the same horizontal level as the applicant's eyes.

Lighting, Eyes, and Facial Expression Standards

Lighting must be uniform and diffused across the entire face and background. Natural daylight near a window or professional studio lighting is recommended. No flash reflections, no red-eye, no shadows on the face or neck, and no overexposure that washes out skin tone are acceptable. The eyes must be open, clearly visible, and directed straight at the camera. The expression must be neutral with the mouth firmly closed. While a very slight natural relaxed expression is tolerated, any visible smile, frown, raised brow, or open mouth is grounds for rejection.

Glasses, Clothing, and Accessories Policy 2026

Eyeglasses are not permitted for Swiss passport photos in 2026 — this aligns with updated Schengen biometric standards that restrict any eyewear that could obstruct facial recognition mapping, cause glare, or create frame shadows over the eyes. Sunglasses, tinted glasses, and colored contact lenses are strictly forbidden. Clothing should be everyday attire in plain, neutral colors — uniforms, costumes, and official attire are prohibited. Avoid clothing that is the same shade as the light gray background, as it may cause blending. Head coverings are only permitted for documented religious or medical reasons; a signed statement may be required, and the full face from forehead to chin must be clearly visible with no shadows cast.

Children and Infant Photo Rules

Children must follow the same dimensional and expression rules as adults. For infants who cannot hold their head upright, they may be photographed lying on a plain light gray or white sheet from directly above. The child must appear alone in the photo — no adult hands, car seats, or other objects should be visible. For very young infants, partially open eyes are acceptable, but for all other children, eyes must be fully open.

Switzerland Schengen Visa Photo Requirements 2026

Switzerland is a member of the Schengen Area and processes visa applications through its network of embassies, consulates, and accredited third-party visa application centers — including VFS Global and BLS International — operating in over 60 countries worldwide. Whether you are applying for a Schengen short-stay visa (Type C), a national long-stay visa (Type D), a student visa, or a work permit, your photograph must meet the strict biometric standards set by Swiss authorities and the European Union's shared Schengen photo framework in 2026. Submitting a non-compliant photo is one of the most common and easily avoidable causes of visa application rejection.

The required photo size for all Swiss visa applications is 35mm wide × 45mm tall. For digital online submissions via Swiss consulate portals or third-party visa centers, the image must be in JPEG or JPG format with a maximum file size of 120KB. The digital file must maintain sufficient resolution to display all facial features clearly — a minimum of 300 DPI is recommended for print-ready versions. Two printed photographs are generally required for physical visa applications. The distance from the top edge of the photograph to the top of the applicant's head must be exactly 3mm, ensuring consistent biometric framing across all Schengen member applications.

Background Color — Schengen Gray Standard 2026

The background for Swiss Schengen visa photos must be plain neutral gray — not white, not off-white, not cream. This is a specific Schengen Area requirement that differs from many non-European countries. The background must have no shadows cast by the applicant, no visible patterns or textures, no other persons or objects, and must not contrast excessively with the applicant's clothing or skin tone. Adequate distance between the applicant and the background surface (ideally 1–1.5 meters) helps prevent unwanted shadows.

Head Size, Facial Geometry, and Centering

The face must occupy 70% to 80% of the total photo height, with the head measuring between 32mm and 36mm from the top of the skull (excluding hair) to the bottom of the chin. The face width from ear to ear must measure between 16mm and 20mm. Applications will be rejected if the face height falls below 27mm or exceeds 40mm. The applicant must be perfectly centered in the frame — no leaning, rotating, or tilting the head in any direction. Shoulders should be visible at the bottom of the frame, and the camera must be held at the same level as the eyes.

Lighting Quality and Photo Sharpness

Lighting must be uniform, bright, and even across the entire face and background. Natural daylight near a window on a bright day is ideal. The photo must have no shadows on the face or neck, no red-eye, no flash glare or overexposure, and must accurately represent the applicant's natural skin tone without discoloration. The image must be sharp, in precise focus, and free of motion blur, pixelation, or compression artifacts. Blurry, washed-out, or pixelated photos are automatically rejected by both digital portals and manual consulate reviewers.

Expression, Eyes, and Head Position

The facial expression must be completely neutral — mouth firmly closed, no smiling, no frowning, no raised eyebrows. The eyes must be open, clearly visible, and directed straight ahead into the camera lens. Any obstruction of the eyes by hair, glasses frames, or accessories is grounds for rejection. The head must be held straight and level — no chin tilted upward or downward, no sideways rotation.

Glasses, Accessories, and Clothing Rules 2026

Eyeglasses are only permitted in exceptional cases where the applicant has a documented medical requirement — and even then, the lenses must not cause any glare or reflections, and the frames must not obstruct any part of the eyes. In practice, Swiss consulates in 2026 strongly advise removing all eyewear to avoid rejection. Sunglasses, fashion glasses, tinted lenses, and colored contact lenses are strictly forbidden. Head coverings are only allowed for verified religious or medical reasons and must not shadow any part of the face. Clothing should be plain, everyday attire in neutral colors — avoid wearing gray tones that match the background, and uniforms of any kind are not permitted. Jewelry and facial piercings are acceptable as long as they do not obscure any facial features.

Face Size, Positioning, and Head Alignment

The head must be centered within the frame, positioned straight on with no tilting or rotation to either side. Both edges of the face must be clearly visible, and both eyes must be open, level, and directed straight at the camera. Hair must not drape over or obscure the eyes or forehead. The photo must show the head and partial shoulders — it is a close-up portrait, not a full-body image.

Background and Lighting Requirements

The background must be plain white or off-white with no patterns, textures, gradients, or objects. Any shadows falling on the background or across the face will cause the photo to be rejected. Even and balanced lighting across the face is essential — overexposed photos (where facial features are washed out) and underexposed photos (where features appear too dark) are both unacceptable. Standing approximately one meter away from the background wall when the photo is taken helps prevent shadow problems.

Glasses, Headwear, and Appearance Rules

Glasses may officially be worn in Thai passport photos, but the Department of Consular Affairs strongly advises their removal to eliminate the risk of rejection caused by glare, thick frames obscuring facial features, or tinted lenses. If glasses are kept on, there must be zero glare on the lenses, the frames must be thin, and no tint is permitted. Sunglasses are never allowed. Hats and headwear are prohibited except for religious or medical head coverings — in those cases, the face must remain completely unobstructed from chin to forehead. Earphones, headsets, and similar accessories are not permitted. Uniforms should not be worn.

Color, Format, and Print Quality

The photo must be in color — black and white photographs are not accepted for Thai passport applications. The print must be of continuous-tone photographic quality. Ordinary inkjet printer printouts are not appropriate for consular submission. The image must be sharp, in focus, and free of pixelation, blur, red-eye, or any visible digital manipulation. Brightness and contrast must accurately represent the applicant's natural appearance.

Children, Infants, and Applicants Under 20

Passport regulations apply to Thai nationals of all ages, including newborns, who each require their own individual passport and a separate photo taken within the last six months. For infants, toys, bottles, pacifiers, and other objects must not appear in the photo. If a parent must hold the infant, hands and arms may not be visible in the frame. Placing the infant on a white cloth on a flat surface is a practical approach to achieve the required white background. Applicants under 20 years of age must be accompanied by both parents when applying at a Thai Embassy or Consulate; they are not eligible to apply online independently.

Important: Passport Production and Delivery

All Thai e-passports are produced centrally by the Department of Consular Affairs in Bangkok and distributed by airmail to embassies and consulates worldwide. Thai nationals aged 20 and over may apply for passport renewal online through their nearest Thai Embassy, receiving the completed passport by mail. Online renewal is not available for applicants under 20 years old.

Thailand's Ministry of Foreign Affairs (กระทรวงการต่างประเทศ) unified all Thai visa applications into a single electronic system starting January 1, 2025. All visa categories — Tourist (TR), Non-Immigrant, Transit, and others — are now processed through the official Thai e-Visa portal at thaievisa.go.th, operated by the Department of Consular Affairs. Whether you are applying for a Single-Entry Tourist Visa, a Multiple-Entry Tourist Visa, a Non-Immigrant B (business), or any other category, your photo must meet the same core biometric standards. A non-compliant photo is one of the most frequent reasons the consular officer returns a visa application for correction, delaying the process by several business days.

Thailand Visa Photo — Official Specifications 2026

Size
35 × 45 mm
Orientation
Portrait (vertical)
Head Coverage
70% – 80%
Background
Plain White
Color
Color only
Expression
Neutral, mouth closed
Selfies
Not accepted
Photo Age
Within 6 months

The Thai e-Visa System: Unified from January 2025

Since January 1, 2025, all Royal Thai Embassies and Consulates worldwide process visa applications exclusively through thaievisa.go.th. This means there is no longer a separate in-person paper application process at most Thai missions — the photo you submit must be a digital upload that meets biometric standards. The photo must be in portrait (vertical) orientation, in JPEG or PNG format, with the full head from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin clearly visible. The head height must be approximately 70% of the photo's total height. The face must be front-facing, centered, and looking directly at the camera.

Background, Lighting, and Shadows

The background must be plain white or off-white with absolutely no patterns, shadows, furniture, or objects visible. Shadows across the face or on the background behind the head are a primary reason for photo rejection by Thai consular officers. To avoid background shadows, stand or sit at least 50 cm away from the wall when the photo is taken, and use soft, even lighting. Overexposed images — where the background bleeds into the face — and underexposed images — where facial features are obscured — are both rejected. Even, diffused lighting that accurately renders skin tone is the standard.

Glasses and Headwear Rules

Glasses are officially permitted in Thai visa photos provided the frames do not obstruct the eyes, there is no glare on the lenses, and the lenses are not tinted. However, given the risk of rejection, removing glasses is the strongly recommended approach. Sunglasses are never acceptable. Hats and headwear are not permitted except for religious or medical head coverings worn as part of daily practice — even then, the face must be fully unobstructed from chin to forehead. Earphones, headsets, and accessories of any kind are not allowed. Photos must show a neutral expression with the mouth closed and teeth not visible.

Photo Age and Color Requirements

The photo must have been taken within the last six months. Using an older photo — even one that is otherwise technically correct — is a grounds for rejection, particularly on renewal applications where the officer can compare against the previous visa photo. The photo must be in color; black and white or sepia photos are not accepted under any circumstances.

Selfies, Perspective, and Who Takes the Photo

Selfies are not acceptable for Thai visa applications. The camera should be positioned at eye level, approximately one meter from the subject, to ensure correct facial proportions and to avoid the perspective distortion that a selfie or close-range photo introduces. A tripod or a second person taking the photo achieves the correct result. The applicant must be alone in the photo — no other people, hands, or objects may be visible in the frame.

Children and Infants Applying for Thai Visas

Children and infants require their own individual visa and their own individual photo. The same background, lighting, and expression rules apply. Toys, bottles, and pacifiers must not appear in the frame. If a parent must hold the infant, hands and arms cannot be visible. Placing the infant on a white cloth provides the required background. For children, the head should still occupy 70–80% of the photo height where possible, though the Thai authorities allow some flexibility on head-size ratios for very young children.

Apply Through the Official Portal Only

The only official website for Thai visa applications is thaievisa.go.th, operated by the Department of Consular Affairs, Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Kingdom of Thailand. Applicants should not use third-party websites that claim to offer Thai visa services, as these are not affiliated with the Thai government. The e-Visa fee is non-refundable once paid, regardless of the outcome of the application.

Türk Pasaportu Biyometrik Fotoğraf Gereksinimleri 2026

Türkiye Cumhuriyeti biyometrik pasaportu için fotoğraf standartları, T.C. Dışişleri Bakanlığı ve Nüfus ve Vatandaşlık İşleri Genel Müdürlüğü tarafından belirlenmektedir. 2026 yılı itibarıyla bu standartlar, yurt içinde nüfus müdürlüklerine yapılan başvurularda ve yurt dışındaki Türk konsolosluklarında aynı şekilde uygulanmaktadır. Pasaport başvurunuzun ilk seferde onaylanması için fotoğrafınızın aşağıdaki zorunlu teknik özellikleri tam olarak karşılaması gerekmektedir.

Resmi Boyut ve Format Gereksinimleri

Türk biyometrik pasaportu için kabul edilen tek fotoğraf boyutu 50 mm × 60 mm'dir. Bu boyut, birçok ülkede kullanılan standart 35×45 mm pasaport fotoğrafından belirgin biçimde farklıdır; bu nedenle genel amaçlı pasaport fotoğraf hizmetlerine başvurmak ret riskini artırır. T.C. Dışişleri Bakanlığı Konsolosluğu'nun 4 Eylül 2024 tarihli resmi bildirisine göre, ABD'de 2×2 inç (yaklaşık 51×51 mm) boyutundaki fotoğraflar istisna olarak kabul edilmektedir; ancak Türkiye'de yapılacak başvurularda yalnızca 50×60 mm geçerlidir. Fotoğrafın dijital çözünürlüğü baskı kalitesini doğrudan etkiler; 300 DPI baskıda 590×709 piksel karşılık gelir. Dosya formatı JPEG olmalıdır.

50 × 60 mm

İfade

Arka Plan ve Işık Koşulları

T.C. Dışişleri Bakanlığı'nın resmi kılavuzuna göre fotoğraf arka planının düz beyaz ve desensiz olması zorunludur. Gri veya krem rengi arka planlar kabul edilmez. Işıklandırma, yüzün tamamının açıkça görülebilecek şekilde yeterli parlaklıkta olmasını sağlamalı; aynı zamanda aşırı parlaklık, gölge veya yansımadan kaçınılmalıdır. Arka plan üzerinde ya da yüz üzerinde oluşan gölgeler fotoğrafın reddedilmesine neden olur. Keskinlik ve kontrast ayarlarının doğru yapılması, yüzün bulanık görünmemesi için kritik öneme sahiptir; odak tamamen yüz üzerinde olmalıdır.

Yüz Konumu ve Poz Gereksinimleri

Fotoğrafta yüzün fotoğraf yüksekliğinin yüzde yetmişi ile seksenini kaplaması gerekmektedir. Başın öne eğilmemesi ya da yana dönmemesi; kameraya dik, tam cepheden bakılması zorunludur. Gözler tamamen açık olmalı ve doğrudan kameraya bakmalıdır. İfade nötr olmalı, dudaklar kapalı tutulmalıdır; gülümseme kabul edilmez. Saçlar yüzü kapatmamalı, kulaklar mümkünse görünür olmalıdır. Fotoğraf, yüksek kaliteli bir kamerayla çekilmeli ve kaliteli fotoğraf kağıdına basılmalıdır; yırtık, buruşuk ya da ezik fotoğraflar kesinlikle kabul edilmez.

Aksesuar ve Kıyafet Kuralları

Resmi kılavuz, gözlük, küpe, şapka ve başlık gibi aksesuarların kullanımını açıkça yasaklamaktadır; bunlar yüzün görünürlüğünü engelleyen her türlü kıyafet ve aksesuar ile birlikte reddedilir. Yalnızca belgelenmiş tıbbi gereklilik durumunda gözlük takılabilir. Dini amaçlı başörtüsü veya baş örtüsü kabul edilir; ancak bu durumda uygulamanın dini niteliğini doğrulayan imzalı bir beyan sunulması gerekir ve yüzün hiçbir bölümü örtülmemelidir. Tıbbi nedenle baş örtüsü kullanılıyorsa imzalı bir doktor beyanı eklenmelidir.

Fotoğraf Tazeliği

Fotoğrafın başvuru tarihinden en fazla altı ay önce çekilmiş olması ve başvuru sahibinin güncel görünümünü yansıtması gerekmektedir. Saç rengi, yüz şekli veya diğer belirgin özelliklerde önemli değişiklikler olması durumunda yeni bir fotoğraf çekilmesi zorunludur.

Çocuklar ve Bebekler İçin Fotoğraf Kuralları

Resmi kılavuza göre yukarıdaki tüm kurallar çocuklar ve bebekler için de geçerlidir. Fotoğrafta yalnızca başvurulan çocuk yer almalıdır; ebeveyn ya da başka bir kişi görünmemelidir. Bebek koltuğu, oyuncak veya benzeri ekipman fotoğrafta bulunmamalıdır. Başını tutamayan bebek ve küçük çocuklar için düz beyaz ya da kırık beyaz bir bez veya yüzey üzerine sırt üstü yatırılarak fotoğraf çekilebilir.

Nüfus Müdürlüğü İçin Onaylı Pasaport Fotoğrafı

Türk pasaportu başvurusunda fotoğraf nedeniyle ret yaşamamak için boyut, arka plan, ifade ve aksesuar kurallarına eksiksiz uymak gerekir. Yapay zeka destekli aracımız, T.C. Dışişleri Bakanlığı'nın 4 Eylül 2024 tarihli resmi biyometrik fotoğraf kılavuzuna uygun olarak yüzü 50×60 mm'lik alana otomatik olarak hizalar, arka planı saf beyaza dönüştürür ve JPEG formatında 300 DPI çözünürlükte teslim eder. Tek seferde, garantili sonuç.

Hızlı Kontrol Listesi — Türk Pasaport Fotoğrafı

  • Boyut: 50 × 60 mm (yurt dışında: 2×2 inç konsolosluklarda kabul edilir).
  • Arka Plan: Düz, desensiz, saf beyaz — gri veya krem kabul edilmez.
  • Yüz Oranı: Fotoğraf yüksekliğinin %70–%80'i.
  • İfade: Nötr, ağız ve dudaklar kapalı.
  • Gözler: Tamamen açık, kameraya dik bakış.
  • Baş Pozisyonu: Öne eğilmeden ya da yana dönmeden tam cephe.
  • Aksesuar: Gözlük, şapka, başlık yasak (tıbbi/dini istisna ile beyan zorunlu).
  • Tazelik: Son 6 ay içinde çekilmiş.
  • Kalite: Buruşuk, yırtık veya hasarlı fotoğraf kabul edilmez.
  • Format: JPEG, 300 DPI baskı kalitesi.

Türkiye Vize Fotoğrafı Gereksinimleri 2026

Türkiye'ye turistik, ticari veya diğer amaçlarla vize başvurusu yaparken sunulan fotoğrafın T.C. Dışişleri Bakanlığı'nın belirlediği biyometrik standartları karşılaması zorunludur. Bu standartlar, konsolosluklara yapılan vize başvurularında ve evisa.gov.tr üzerinden gerçekleştirilen çevrimiçi e-Vize başvurularında birlikte uygulanmaktadır. Fotoğraf, başvurunun hem ilk inceleme hem de biyometrik doğrulama aşamalarında değerlendirildiğinden, teknik özelliklerin eksiksiz karşılanması ret riskini doğrudan ortadan kaldırır.

Resmi Boyut ve Teknik Özellikler

T.C. Dışişleri Bakanlığı Konsolosluğu'nun 4 Eylül 2024 tarihli resmi biyometrik fotoğraf kılavuzuna göre vize başvurusunda kullanılan fotoğrafın boyutu 50 mm × 60 mm olmalıdır. ABD konsolosluklarına yapılan başvurularda 2×2 inç boyutu istisna olarak kabul edilmektedir; ancak Türkiye'deki vize merkezlerine ve büyükelçiliklere yapılan başvurularda yalnızca 50×60 mm kabul edilir. Standart 35×45 mm pasaport fotoğrafları Türkiye vize başvurusunda geçersizdir ve başvurunun reddedilmesine yol açar. Dijital başvurularda dosya formatının JPEG olması ve konsolosluk portalının belirlediği boyut sınırına — genellikle 10 MB altı — uyulması gerekir.

Yüz, Baş Pozisyonu ve İfade

Baş öne eğilmemeli ya da yana dönmemelidir; kameraya tam cepheden, dik olarak bakılmalıdır. Gözler tamamen açık olmalı ve doğrudan kameraya odaklanmış olmalıdır. İfade nötr olmalı, dudaklar kapalı tutulmalıdır. Gülümseme veya abartılı yüz ifadesi kabul edilmez. Saçlar yüzü örtmemelidir.

Aksesuar ve Kıyafet

Gözlük, küpe, şapka ve başlık dahil yüzün görünürlüğünü engelleyebilecek her türlü aksesuar yasaktır. Yalnızca belgelenmiş tıbbi gereklilik durumunda gözlük kullanılabilir. Dini amaçlı başörtüsü, yüzün tamamı açık olması ve uygulamanın dini niteliğini doğrulayan imzalı beyanın başvuruya eklenmesi koşuluyla kabul edilir. Tıbbi nedenle baş örtüsü kullanılıyorsa imzalı doktor beyanı zorunludur. Günlük kıyafet tercih edilmeli, üniforma, kostüm veya resmi kıyafet andıran giysilerden kaçınılmalıdır.

e-Vize Dijital Başvurusu

Türkiye'nin resmi çevrimiçi e-Vize portalı evisa.gov.tr üzerinden yapılan başvurularda fotoğraf dijital olarak yüklenmelidir. Dijital dosya JPEG formatında olmalı ve konsolosluk portalının teknik gerekliliklerine — genellikle 10 MB altı dosya boyutu — uyulmalıdır. Portalın piksel kısıtlamalarını aşmamak için fotoğrafın 590×709 piksel olması önerilir. Sahte ve resmi olmayan e-Vize sitelerinden kaçınılmalı; yalnızca evisa.gov.tr kullanılmalıdır.

Fiziksel Başvurularda Fotoğraf Sayısı

Konsolosluk veya vize başvuru merkezi aracılığıyla yapılan fiziksel vize başvurularında iki adet özdeş renkli fotoğraf sunulması gerekmektedir. Fotoğraflar yüksek kaliteli fotoğraf kağıdına basılmış olmalı; yırtık, buruşuk ya da hasarlı fotoğraflar kabul edilmez.

Çocuklar İçin Kurallar

Her çocuğun ayrı bir vize başvurusu yapması ve bu başvuruya çocuğa ait ayrı bir fotoğraf eklenmesi gerekmektedir. Grup fotoğrafı ve eski tarihli fotoğraf kabul edilmez. Yukarıda belirtilen tüm teknik özellikler çocuklar için de aynen geçerlidir.

Sorunsuz Türkiye Vize Başvurusu İçin Doğru Fotoğraf

Türkiye vize başvurunuzda fotoğraf nedeniyle gecikme veya ret yaşamamak için T.C. Dışişleri Bakanlığı'nın belirlediği 50×60 mm boyutu, saf beyaz arka plan ve biyometrik yüz oranı kurallarına eksiksiz uyulması zorunludur. Yapay zeka destekli aracımız, Türk konsolosluklarında ve evisa.gov.tr portalında geçerli JPEG formatında, 300 DPI kalitesinde, doğru boyutlarda vize fotoğrafı oluşturur. Resmi kaynak: T.C. Dışişleri Bakanlığı biyometrik fotoğraf kılavuzu, 4 Eylül 2024.

Hızlı Kontrol Listesi — Türkiye Vize Fotoğrafı

  • Boyut: 50 × 60 mm — standart 35×45 mm kabul edilmez.
  • Arka Plan: Düz, desensiz, saf beyaz.
  • Yüz Oranı: Fotoğraf yüksekliğinin %70–%80'i.
  • İfade: Nötr, ağız ve dudaklar kapalı.
  • Gözler: Tamamen açık, kameraya dik bakış.
  • Baş Pozisyonu: Tam cephe, öne eğilmeden veya yana dönmeden.
  • Aksesuar: Gözlük, şapka, küpe yasak (tıbbi/dini istisna beyanı zorunlu).
  • Tazelik: Son 6 ay içinde çekilmiş.
  • Adet: Fiziksel başvuruda 2 adet özdeş renkli baskı.
  • Dijital Format: JPEG, evisa.gov.tr için 10 MB altı.
  • Baskı Kalitesi: Yüksek kaliteli fotoğraf kağıdı; hasarlı fotoğraf kabul edilmez.

name: United Kingdom Passport
passportcontent:
UK Biometric Passport: Official HMPO Photo Requirements 2026

His Majesty's Passport Office (HMPO) sets the photo standards for all British passport applications — whether you are applying for the first time, renewing, or adding pages. The physical print size is 35 × 45 mm (width × height), the standard used in all UK photo booths. For online applications through GOV.UK, a digital file is required instead: minimum 600 × 750 pixels, between 50 KB and 10 MB, in JPEG format. HMPO updated its Photo Standards to Version 49.0 on 8 December 2025, and those rules are fully in force for all 2026 applications.

UK Passport — Official HMPO Specifications

Print Size
35 × 45 mm
Head Height
29–34 mm
Background
Cream / Light Grey
Digital Min
600 × 750 px
File Size
50 KB – 10 MB
Recency
≤ 1 Month
Printed Copies
2 Identical
Format
Colour JPEG

The One-Month Recency Rule — UK's Strictest Requirement

The single most important rule that separates UK passport photos from those of almost every other country: your photo must have been taken within the last month before you submit your application. This applies to all applicants — adults, children, and infants — for both new applications and renewals. GOV.UK states this explicitly: a photo taken more than one month before submission will cause your application to be delayed. This is considerably stricter than the six-month window used by most other countries, and HMPO's December 2025 update tightened enforcement of this rule further. Do not take your photo weeks in advance while gathering other documents — photograph yourself as close to your submission date as possible.

Background: Cream or Light Grey — Not White

GOV.UK's printed photo guidance specifies the background must be plain cream or light grey. For digital uploads, the requirement is a plain light-coloured background. This is one of the most frequently misunderstood rules: applicants used to the pure white background required by the US and most Schengen countries often use a white wall and get flagged by HMPO's automated checker. The GOV.UK digital submission system uses automated photo-checking technology that is particularly sensitive to background contrast. A plain light grey background produces the most consistent pass rate with HMPO's automated system. The background must be completely plain — no patterns, no gradients, no visible furniture, and no shadows behind you.

Head Size: 29–34 mm Chin to Crown

The image of your face — measured from the bottom of your chin to the top of your head (crown) — must be between 29 mm and 34 mm within the 45 mm tall frame. This translates to approximately 65–75% of the photo height. This range is tighter than most applicants expect. HMPO figures show that around one in five online applications is affected by a photo problem, and incorrect head sizing is among the most common causes of rejection. Your eyes must be open and clearly visible, and your hair must not fall in front of your eyes.

Glasses: Avoid Unless Medically Necessary

GOV.UK's current guidance states clearly: do not wear glasses in your photo unless you have to do so. If you must wear glasses for a documented medical reason, they cannot be sunglasses or tinted glasses, and your eyes must not be covered by the frames or obscured by any glare, reflection, or shadow. HMPO strongly recommends removing glasses to prevent automatic rejection by its photo-checking system. Head coverings are not allowed except for religious or medical reasons, and even then, your full face must remain visible from chin to forehead.

Expression, Pose, and Clothing

Per GOV.UK, your photo must show a plain expression with your mouth closed. Both eyes must be open and looking directly at the camera. You must face forwards — no tilting or turning. Your full head and upper shoulders must be visible, and the photo must contain no other people or objects. There are no restrictions on everyday clothing, but clothing must be in clear contrast to the background. Uniforms are not prohibited by HMPO's photo rules, though the general guidance is to wear normal attire.

Paper vs. Online Applications in 2026

Paper applications require 2 identical printed photos submitted loose with your form. Online applications through GOV.UK require a single digital upload, or you can enter a photo code obtained from a photo booth or shop (such as Timpson, Snappy Snaps, or Max Spielmann) — these services transmit the photo directly to HMPO. The online application fee is £94.50; the paper form costs £107, following the April 2025 fee increase. Photos taken with your own device are accepted for online applications but are described by GOV.UK as less likely to be approved than photos from a booth or shop.

Compliance Checklist — HMPO Photo Standards v49.0 (December 2025)

  • Size: 35 × 45 mm printed; 600–900 × 750–1125 px digital.
  • Background: Plain cream or light grey (printed); plain light-coloured (digital). No pure white, no patterns, no shadows.
  • Head Height: 29–34 mm chin to crown within the frame.
  • Recency: Taken within the last month — not 6 months.
  • Expression: Plain/neutral, mouth closed, eyes open, looking straight at the camera.
  • Hair: Must not cover eyes or face.
  • Glasses: Remove unless medically required. No tinted lenses, no glare.
  • Head Coverings: Only for religious or medical reasons; full face must remain visible.
  • No shadows on face or background.
  • No digital alterations — unaltered colour photo on plain white photographic paper (for print).
  • Printed copies: 2 identical, separated and loose (paper applications).

One in Five Applications Delayed by Photo Problems

HMPO data shows approximately one in five online passport applications encounters a photo issue. The most common causes are an incorrect background colour (white instead of cream or light grey), head size outside the 29–34 mm range, and photos taken more than one month before submission. Our AI tool validates all three against the HMPO Photo Standards v49.0 before you upload, producing a GOV.UK-ready file that meets every technical requirement for a 2026 British passport application.

UK Visa Photo Requirements 2026: UKVI Official Standards

Photo requirements for UK visa applications are set by UK Visas and Immigration (UKVI), which sits within the Home Office. Whether you are applying for a Standard Visitor Visa, a Student Visa, a Skilled Worker Visa, or any other UK immigration category, the photo specification is the same: 35 × 45 mm, plain light-coloured background, neutral expression, and taken within the last month. These rules align with the HMPO biometric standards and the International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) Document 9303 framework used across the UK's border systems.

UK Visa — Photo Specifications (UKVI 2026)

Size
35 × 45 mm
Head Height
29–34 mm
Background
Cream / Light Grey
Recency
≤ 1 Month
Copies Required
2 Identical
Format
Colour Print
Photo Paper
Professional
Glasses
Not Recommended

Background Colour: Cream or Light Grey

The UK visa photo background must be plain cream or light grey — the same standard applied to HMPO passport photos. A pure white background, which is required for US, Schengen, and many other visa applications, is not the preferred background for UK documents. This distinction catches many applicants off guard. The background must be completely plain with no patterns, objects, or shadows. Any shadow behind you — even a faint one — is grounds for rejection. The photo must also show a clear contrast between your face and the background, which is why a plain light grey works reliably across all skin tones.

The One-Month Rule Applies to Visas Too

Just as with UK passport photos, your visa photo must have been taken within the last month before you submit your application. This is one of the strictest recency requirements of any major immigration authority globally. Do not reuse a photo taken for another purpose, even a recent one — if it was taken more than four weeks before your visa submission date, it will not be compliant. Two identical copies must be submitted with paper-based visa applications.

Face, Expression, and Head Positioning

Your face must be centred in the frame and take up 65–75% of the photo height — corresponding to a chin-to-crown measurement of 29–34 mm within the 45 mm frame. You must face directly forward, with both eyes open, clearly visible, and looking straight at the camera. Your expression must be plain and neutral, with your mouth closed. Hair must not fall in front of your eyes. The full head and upper shoulders must be visible — the photo is not just of the face.

Glasses, Head Coverings, and Special Cases

UK visa guidance mirrors HMPO passport rules: do not wear glasses unless you have a documented medical necessity. If glasses must be worn, the lenses must not be tinted, the frames must not cover the eyes, and there must be no glare or reflection. Head coverings are not permitted except for religious or medical reasons, and even then, the complete face from chin to top of forehead — and both sides from ear to ear — must be fully visible. Babies and young children follow relaxed rules: under-ones do not need to have their eyes open, and a supporting hand may be used provided it is not visible in the photo.

Photo Quality and Printing Standards

Visa photos must be printed to a professional standard on plain photographic paper, in colour, and in clear focus. The photo must not be creased, torn, or marked. No computer software alterations are permitted — this includes the removal of blemishes, the adjustment of skin tone, or any other post-processing that changes your natural appearance. The photo must not have red-eye. Both copies submitted must be identical.

Uniform Standard Across All UK Visa Categories

UKVI applies the same 35 × 45 mm photo specification to all visa categories — Standard Visitor, Student, Skilled Worker, Family, and all other routes. There is no variation in photo requirements based on the type of visa you are applying for. The only differences lie in the number of supporting documents and the fee structure. For the most current official requirements, UKVI guidance is published at gov.uk/uk-visa-photo-requirements.

Visa Photo Compliance Checklist

  • Size: 35 × 45 mm, colour, professional print quality.
  • Background: Plain cream or light grey. No pure white, no shadows, no patterns.
  • Head Height: 29–34 mm chin to crown (65–75% of frame).
  • Recency: Taken within the last month before submission.
  • Expression: Plain/neutral, mouth closed, eyes open, facing forward.
  • Hair: Must not cover eyes or any part of the face.
  • Glasses: Avoid unless medically required; no tinted lenses, no glare.
  • Head Covering: Only religious or medical exemptions; full face must be visible.
  • Copies: 2 identical prints, unattached and loose.
  • No alterations: Unretouched, no filters, no digital enhancements.

Get Your UK Visa Photo Right First Time in 2026

UK visa photo rejections are most commonly caused by the wrong background colour, incorrect head sizing, photos older than one month, or glasses worn without medical justification. Our AI tool applies the same checks used by UKVI-compliant processors — validating background shade, chin-to-crown measurement, and recency compliance — so your 35 × 45 mm photo is submission-ready for any UK visa category in 2026.

Decoding the CEAC Upload Requirements (2026 Update)

When you reach the photo upload page of the DS-160 or DS-260 application, you are interacting with the Consular Electronic Application Center (CEAC). This system has some of the most rigid technical barriers of any government portal. If your file is 1 pixel off or 1 KB too heavy, you will be blocked from proceeding. In this 2026 guide, we provide the ultimate technical cheat sheet for the **digital US visa photo specs**, ensuring your file enters the system flawlessly on the first attempt.

The Technical Target

File Format
JPEG (.jpg)
Dimensions
600x600 px
Max Size
240 KB
Color Space
24-bit sRGB

The JPEG Compression Paradox

The government requires a photo that is both large enough for clarity (at least 600x600px) and small enough for their aging servers (under 240KB). Most photo editing software sacrifices quality for size. To stay under the limit without triggering a 'Quality Alert' (which happens when an image is too blurry from compression), you must use 'Smart Compression' that prioritizes the biometric data in the center of the image. Our tool handles this automatically, delivering the perfect balance of size and sharp resolution.

Advanced Biometric Rules: Head Sizing and Eyeline

The U.S. Department of State uses facial recognition software that relies on the "Golden Triangle" - the relationship between your eyes, nose, and mouth. If your head is too small or too large in the frame, the algorithm cannot calculate these distances accurately.

  • Head Height: Your head (from chin to top of hair) must be between 50% and 69% of the total height of the image.
  • Eye Height: Your eyes must be between 56% and 69% of the image's height from the bottom edge.
  • Digital Centering: The center of your head must be perfectly aligned with the vertical axis of the 600x600 grid.

Pro Tip: The 'Eyeline' Rule

Most rejections happen because the eyes are too high in the frame. This usually occurs when people crop the photo manually to 'look good' rather than to 'be compliant'. Our AI automatically anchors your eyes at the 60% line, which is the 'sweet spot' for CEAC approval.

Lighting and Shadows: The 'Invisible' Rejection Cause

Uniform lighting is mandatory. Shadowing on the face or background is the #1 cause of manual rejection by consular officers. The US Visa portal pre-screens for contrast; if the background isn't a pure, neutral white (#FFFFFF), the automated validator will likely flag it as 'Background Not Uniform'. Our tool uses neural networks to extrude your silhouette and inject a digitally clean white backdrop that meets 2026 department standards.

Dress Code and Accessories for US Visa Photos

The rules for what to wear are just as strict as the pixel counts. As of 2026, the following rules are strictly enforced:

  • No Eyeglasses: Even if you wear them every day, they are 100% prohibited. The only exception is a signed medical statement for highly specific cases (rarely granted).
  • Uniforms are Prohibited: Do not wear anything that looks like military, police, or corporate uniforms. Work attire is fine, but avoid anything that looks like an official designation.
  • Head Coverings: Only allowed for religious or medical reasons, provided they do not obscure any part of the face. Your hairline must be visible if possible.
  • Neutral Expression: While a 'natural smile' is technically allowed, consulate officers prefer a neutral expression with both eyes open. Avoid 'toothy' smiles which can distort your jawline biometrics.

Technical Meta-Data and Color Depth

Beyond what you see, the CEAC system checks file metadata. The image must be in 24-bit sRGB color space. Grayscale images, even if they look high quality, will be rejected immediately. Our processing engine strips away non-essential EXIF data that can sometimes block uploads, while ensuring the color profile remains strictly compliant with the 2026 U.S. Visa specifications.

Pasaporte Venezolano: Requisitos de Fotografía

El pasaporte venezolano, tramitado por el Servicio Administrativo de Identificación, Migración y Extranjería (SAIME), requiere una fotografía de tamaño 30x40mm. Este es un formato pequeño y específico. La foto debe tener un fondo blanco. El requisito más estricto es el tamaño de la cabeza, que debe ocupar exactamente el 75% de la altura de la foto. No se aceptan fotos con un rango (como 70-80%), sino que debe ser precisamente el 75%.

El Desafío del 75% Exacto

Lograr exactamente el 75% de altura de la cabeza en un marco de 30x40mm es extremadamente difícil sin herramientas profesionales. Si la cabeza es ligeramente más pequeña (por ejemplo, 73%) o más grande (77%), el sistema SAIME rechazará la foto. Nuestra IA mide con precisión milimétrica la distancia desde la barbilla hasta la coronilla y escala la imagen para que la cabeza ocupe exactamente el 75% del espacio vertical de 40mm.

  • Tamaño: 30x40mm.
  • Cabeza: Exactamente 75%.
  • Expresión: Neutra, boca cerrada.

Precisión SAIME Garantizada

Evita el rechazo por tamaño de cabeza incorrecto. Nuestra IA crea una foto 30x40mm con la cabeza escalada exactamente al 75% como exige el SAIME.

Yemen Visa Photo Requirements 2026: Digital and Print Standards

Whether you are applying for a Yemeni tourist visa, transit visa, or business visa, every application requires a correctly formatted photograph that meets the standards set by Yemeni consular offices and embassies worldwide. The Yemen visa photo requirement in 2026 specifies a 60×40mm landscape-format image — the same unusual horizontal orientation required for the passport. The background must be pure white with no patterns, gradients, or shadows. The head must fill between 70% and 80% of the photo's height, and the expression must be completely neutral with the mouth closed. Photos that are the wrong size, the wrong orientation, or have a non-white background are rejected at the consular counter without exception, delaying your entire visa application by weeks.

Yemen Visa Photo Specifications 2026

Dimensions
60×40 mm
Background
Pure White
Head Size
70%–80%
Format
JPEG

The Landscape Format Problem: Why Most Photos Are Wrong

The single most common reason for Yemen visa photo rejection is submitting a portrait-orientation photo instead of landscape. Standard passport photo booths, photo apps, and even professional studios default to portrait format for visa photos. Yemen's 60×40mm requirement is landscape — 60mm wide and only 40mm tall. This means if you submit a standard 35×45mm or 51×51mm photo, it will be rejected immediately regardless of how good the photo quality is. The face positioning also changes significantly in landscape format: there is more horizontal space on either side of the face, and the white background must fill this space evenly without any color variation. Our AI tool is specifically calibrated for landscape format output, automatically generating a 1417×945 pixel JPEG that corresponds exactly to 60×40mm at 600 DPI — the resolution specification required for clean printing and digital submission.

Yemen Visa Photo Rules: Consular Office Requirements

Yemeni embassies and consular offices enforce ICAO-aligned biometric standards. This means the photo is not just a formality — it is scanned and cross-referenced against facial recognition databases during the visa processing stage. Any photo that fails automated biometric checks is flagged for manual review or outright rejection. The key biometric parameters are head height ratio (70–80% of vertical frame), eye position (50–65% from the bottom), and interpupillary symmetry — meaning your face must be straight, not tilted left or right by more than two degrees. Our compliance engine checks all of these parameters automatically before you download your photo, giving you a pass/fail report with specific measurements so you know exactly where your photo stands before you submit.

What Happens if Your Yemen Visa Photo Is Rejected

A rejected visa photo does not just mean taking a new photo. It typically means your entire visa application is returned, you lose your appointment slot, and you must restart the submission process from the beginning. In countries where Yemen has limited consular representation, the next available appointment could be months away. This is why getting the photo right on the first attempt is critical. The most common rejection reasons are: wrong dimensions (portrait instead of landscape), background that is off-white or cream rather than pure white, head positioned too low or too high within the frame, and glasses causing glare on the lens. All of these are checked automatically by our tool before you download.

Complete Yemen Visa Photo Checklist 2026

  • Dimensions: 60×40mm landscape format — 60mm wide, 40mm tall, no portrait orientation.
  • Digital file: JPEG at 1417×945 pixels, high quality, minimal compression.
  • Background: Pure white — no cream, grey, blue, or any off-white variation accepted.
  • Head height: Face from chin to top of hair must occupy 70%–80% of the photo height.
  • Eye level: Eyes positioned 50%–65% up from the bottom of the frame.
  • Expression: Completely neutral, mouth closed, no smile whatsoever.
  • Shadows: Zero shadows on the face or background — even, flat lighting required.
  • Glasses: Remove all eyewear — consular offices follow ICAO no-glasses guidelines.
  • Head coverings: Only permitted for documented religious reasons, full face must remain visible.
  • Photo age: Must be taken within the last six months of your application date.

Get Your Yemen Visa Photo Right First Time

Stop guessing whether your photo meets Yemen's unusual landscape format requirement. Upload your photo to our AI tool and receive a fully compliant 60×40mm white-background JPEG in seconds — verified against every Yemeni consular office standard and ready to print or submit digitally.

Yemen Passport Photo Requirements 2026: Complete Compliance Guide

Getting your Yemen passport photo right the first time saves you weeks of costly delays. The Yemeni Immigration, Passport, and Nationality Authority enforces strict biometric photo standards, and any submission that fails to meet even a single requirement is rejected outright. In 2026, Yemen requires a 60×40mm landscape-format photo — meaning the width is greater than the height, which is unusual compared to most countries and catches many applicants off guard. The background must be pure white, the face must be perfectly centered within the frame, and the head must occupy between 70% and 80% of the photo's vertical height. The file format must be JPEG, and the photo must be printed or submitted digitally at the correct resolution. Understanding every one of these rules before you submit is the difference between a smooth application and a frustrating, expensive rejection.

Yemen Passport Photo Specifications 2026

Dimensions
60×40 mm

Pure White
Head Size
70%–80%

Why the Landscape Format Catches Applicants Off Guard

Most passport photos around the world use a portrait orientation — taller than they are wide. Yemen is one of the few countries that requires a landscape (horizontal) format at 60×40mm, with the width being 60mm and the height just 40mm. This means a standard photo booth or smartphone passport photo app will almost certainly produce the wrong dimensions. The face must still be centered horizontally and vertically within this wider frame, with even lighting on both sides. If you crop a portrait-format photo to landscape dimensions, you will almost always cut off the top of the head or lose the required white border. Our AI tool automatically detects your face position and generates the correct 1417×945 pixel digital file that maps precisely to the 60×40mm print requirement at the standard 600 DPI resolution used by Yemeni authorities.

Step-by-Step: How to Take a Yemen Passport Photo at Home

Taking your own Yemen passport photo at home is entirely possible if you follow these steps precisely. First, stand or sit in front of a plain white wall — a sheet of white A4 paper taped to a wall works perfectly if you don't have a white background. Make sure the lighting is even on both sides of your face with no shadows falling across your forehead, cheeks, or chin. Natural daylight from a window directly in front of you is ideal. Ask someone to photograph you from approximately one meter away using a smartphone in landscape mode. Your entire face, from chin to the top of your hair, must be clearly visible with no hair obscuring your eyes or forehead. Wear your everyday clothing — avoid white shirts that could blend into the background. Upload the photo to our tool, and the AI will automatically remove the background, replace it with compliant pure white, center your face, verify the 70–80% head height ratio, and export a print-ready JPEG at 1417×945 pixels.

Expression, Glasses, and Head Coverings: What the Rules Say

Yemeni passport photo rules are explicit about facial expression: you must maintain a completely neutral expression with your mouth closed. No smiling, no raised eyebrows, no squinting. Your eyes must be fully open and looking directly into the camera lens, not at the screen. Glasses are strongly discouraged in 2026 — most Yemeni consular offices now follow ICAO biometric guidelines that reject photos with any form of eyewear due to glare and reflection interference with automated facial recognition systems. If you wear prescription glasses daily, remove them for the photo. Head coverings are only permitted for genuine religious reasons, and even then the full face from chin to forehead and ear to ear must remain completely uncovered and unobstructed. Decorative headwear, hats, or caps of any kind are not permitted.

Complete Yemen Passport Photo Compliance Checklist

  • Dimensions: Exactly 60×40mm landscape format — width greater than height, no exceptions.
  • Digital resolution: 1417×945 pixels at 600 DPI for print-ready submissions.
  • Background: Pure white only — cream, off-white, grey, or light blue backgrounds are rejected.
  • Head size: The head (chin to top of natural hair) must occupy 70%–80% of the photo's vertical height.
  • Eye position: Eyes must be positioned between 50% and 65% up from the bottom of the frame.
  • Expression: Neutral, mouth fully closed, no smile of any degree.
  • Lighting: Even, shadow-free illumination across the entire face and background.
  • Glasses: Not recommended — remove eyewear for guaranteed compliance.
  • File format: JPEG, high quality, no heavy compression artifacts.
  • Recency: Photo must have been taken within the last six months.

Upload any clear photo of your face, and within seconds you will receive a perfectly cropped, white-background, biometric-compliant 60×40mm Yemen passport photo ready to print on A4 or submit digitally to any Yemeni consular office worldwide.
