name: Nepal Passport
passportcontent:
Nepal Passport Photo Requirements (2026)

Planning to apply for or renew a Nepal passport in 2026? Getting your passport photo right the first time saves you from costly delays at the Department of Passports office in Tripureshwor, Kathmandu. This complete guide covers every official requirement you need to know — from photo dimensions and background color to facial expression and print quality.

Photo Size and Dimensions

The official Nepal passport photo size is 35 mm × 45 mm (3.5 cm × 4.5 cm), equivalent to approximately 1.37 × 1.77 inches. At a resolution of 600 DPI (dots per inch), this translates to 413 × 531 pixels. Photos that do not meet these exact dimensions will be rejected at the time of application. You are typically required to submit four (4) identical copies of the photo with your application.

Background Requirements

Your Nepal passport photo must have a plain white or very light grey background with no patterns, textures, shadows, or gradient effects. The background must be uniform throughout the entire image. Any shadows falling on the background from the subject will result in automatic rejection. Make sure the background is well-lit and clearly distinguishable from your face and hair.

Head Size and Positioning

The head — measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin — must occupy between 70% and 80% of the total image height. Your face must be centered within the frame, looking directly at the camera. The head must not be tilted, turned, or rotated in any direction. Both ears should ideally be visible. The top of the shoulders should also be included at the bottom of the frame to provide proper context and proportions.

Facial Expression and Eyes

Maintain a neutral facial expression with your mouth closed. Smiling, frowning, raised eyebrows, or any exaggerated expression is not allowed. Both eyes must be fully open, clearly visible, and looking directly at the camera. Your eyes should be positioned within the upper half of the photo, roughly between 50% and 65% of the image height from the bottom. Blinking or partially closed eyes are grounds for rejection.

Photo Quality and Technical Standards

The photo must be in full color — black-and-white photos are not accepted. The image must be sharp, in focus, and free from pixelation or blurring. There must be no digital alterations, retouching, filters, or color corrections applied. Lighting must be even across the face with no harsh shadows, hot spots, or glare. The print must be on high-quality photo paper — glossy or matte — and the colors must be true to life. For digital submissions, the file format should be JPEG, and the file size should ideally remain under 300 KB.

Clothing and Accessories

Wear your everyday clothing for the photo. Uniforms, costumes, or clothing that resembles any official or military attire is strictly prohibited. Hats and head coverings are generally not allowed, with the only exceptions being those worn for documented religious or medical reasons — in which case a signed statement from a religious authority or a doctor must accompany the application. Eyeglasses may only be worn if required for medical purposes (with documentation); otherwise, they must be removed. Sunglasses are never permitted under any circumstances. Jewelry and small facial accessories are allowed as long as they do not obscure or cast shadows on any part of your face.

Photo Recency

Nepal passport photos must be taken within the last six months of the application date. Photos that are older than six months are considered outdated and will not be accepted. This ensures that the photo accurately reflects the applicant's current appearance.

ePassport vs. MRP Applications

As of 2026, Nepal's Department of Passports has largely transitioned to issuing ePassports (biometric passports) at the central office in Kathmandu. Some District Administration Offices (DAOs) in remote regions may still process Machine Readable Passport (MRP) applications. The photo requirements are identical for both document types: 35 × 45 mm on a white background at high resolution.

Common Reasons for Rejection

The most frequent reasons Nepal passport photos are rejected include: shadows on the background or face, incorrect head size proportions, photo older than six months, background color that is not plain white or light grey, digital enhancements or retouching, poor print quality from low-resolution studio prints, and wearing non-permitted accessories such as sunglasses or hats.

Tips for a Perfect Nepal Passport Photo

Use even, diffused natural lighting or a professional studio setup. Position yourself approximately one metre from the camera. Have someone else take the photo — selfies are not acceptable for official passport applications. Use a tripod for the camera to avoid motion blur. Always verify your photo against the official dimensions and head-size ratios before submitting your application at the Department of Passports.
visacontent:
Nepal Visa Photo Requirements (2026)

Whether you are applying for a Nepal tourist visa, a Nepal visa on arrival at Tribhuvan International Airport (TIA), or submitting an advance application through the official Department of Immigration portal, your visa photo must meet strict official specifications. A non-compliant photo is one of the leading causes of visa application delays and rejections. This comprehensive guide covers every official Nepal visa photo requirement for 2026.

Photo Size and Dimensions

The Nepal visa photo must measure exactly 35 mm × 45 mm (3.5 × 4.5 cm), the same standard size as the Nepal passport photo. This is equivalent to approximately 1.37 × 1.77 inches. Photos submitted for visa on arrival at TIA or via the online immigration portal must conform to these precise dimensions. Incorrect sizing will trigger rejection at the immigration counter or during the online form validation process.

Background Color

The background must be a plain white or very light grey color — uniform, smooth, and completely free of patterns, textures, gradients, or any other distractions. No objects, furniture, or other people should be visible in the background. Shadows cast on the background by the applicant are not acceptable and will lead to the photo being refused. The background should be evenly lit to ensure a clean, professional appearance throughout the entire image.

Head Size and Facial Framing

The head must be centered within the frame and occupy 70% to 80% of the total image height, measured from the crown of the head to the bottom of the chin. The face must be positioned straight and looking directly into the camera. The top of the shoulders must be visible at the lower portion of the frame. Neither the face nor the head should be cut off at any edge of the photograph. Both ears should ideally be visible in the photo.

Facial Expression and Eye Position

A neutral, relaxed facial expression is required — mouth closed, no smiling, no frowning, and no raised eyebrows. Both eyes must be wide open and clearly visible, looking directly at the lens. The eyes should be positioned in the upper half of the image. Ensure there are no reflections or glare on spectacle lenses. Squinting or partially closed eyes will result in the photo being declined.

Photo Quality and Technical Specifications

The Nepal visa photo must be in full color. The image must be sharp, properly focused, and free of blur, noise, or pixelation. No digital editing, retouching, beauty filters, or artificial enhancements are permitted. Lighting must be even across the face — avoid harsh direct flash that creates hot spots or deep shadows around the nose and chin. For digital/online submissions, the photo file format must be JPEG with a minimum resolution of 300 DPI and a maximum file size of 5 MB. For printed physical submissions, use high-quality glossy or matte photo paper to ensure accurate color reproduction and sharpness.

Clothing and Accessories

Wear your regular, everyday clothes that contrast clearly with the white or light grey background. Avoid clothing that is entirely white or very light, as it may blend into the background and create compliance issues. Hats, caps, and head coverings are not permitted, unless worn for verified religious or medical reasons — in both cases, appropriate documentation must be provided, and the full face must remain clearly visible with no shadows. Sunglasses are strictly prohibited. Regular prescription eyeglasses are generally discouraged; if worn, they must not produce glare or obscure the eyes in any way. Accessories such as small jewelry are acceptable provided they do not cast shadows on the face or obstruct facial features.

Children and Infants

Children applying for Nepal visas must have individual photos that meet the same specifications as adults. The photo must show only the child — no hands, toys, or supporting adults should be visible. For infants, closed eyes may be acceptable given the difficulty of keeping very young children still, but open eyes are always preferred. The child's head must be straight and fully visible, with the face centered within the frame.

Photo Recency

Visa photos must have been taken within the past six months of the date of application. This requirement applies to both online applications through the Department of Immigration portal and physical applications submitted at Nepalese embassies or consulates abroad, as well as visa-on-arrival applications at TIA.

Visa on Arrival — Photo Tips

For travelers applying for a Nepal visa on arrival at Tribhuvan International Airport, having your compliant photo prepared in advance is strongly recommended, particularly during peak tourist seasons (spring and autumn) when queues at the immigration kiosks can be long. The kiosk machines at TIA also accept digital photo uploads when completing the arrival form online in advance via the Department of Immigration's official website.

Common Reasons for Nepal Visa Photo Rejection

Frequent rejection reasons include: photo older than six months, incorrect dimensions, colored or patterned background, shadows on the face or background, excessive digital retouching, file size exceeding 5 MB for online submissions, non-JPEG file formats, sunglasses or non-permitted head coverings, and poor print quality on the physical copy.

Summary Checklist

Before submitting your Nepal visa application, verify: ✔ Size is 35 × 45 mm ✔ Background is plain white or very light grey ✔ Head occupies 70–80% of image height ✔ Face is centered, straight, and looking at the camera ✔ Both eyes are open and visible ✔ Neutral expression, mouth closed ✔ No sunglasses or non-religious head coverings ✔ Full color, no retouching or filters ✔ Photo taken within the last 6 months ✔ JPEG format, minimum 300 DPI, under 5 MB for online submission

name: Sri Lanka Passport
passportcontent:
Sri Lanka Passport Photo Requirements (2026)

Submitting the correct passport photo is one of the most critical steps in your Sri Lanka passport application. The Department of Immigration and Emigration of Sri Lanka enforces strict biometric photo standards, and any non-compliant photo will result in your application being rejected or delayed. This complete guide outlines every official requirement you need to meet for a Sri Lanka passport photo in 2026 — from dimensions and background to facial expression and print quality.

Photo Size and Dimensions

The official Sri Lanka passport photo size is 35 mm × 45 mm (3.5 cm × 4.5 cm), equivalent to approximately 1.37 × 1.77 inches. At 600 DPI resolution, this corresponds to 413 × 531 pixels. Photos that deviate from these exact dimensions will not be accepted. Applicants are typically required to submit two to three identical photos along with their application. The photo must be printed on professional-grade continuous-tone photo paper — Polaroid prints and photos produced on standard desktop inkjet or laser printers are explicitly not acceptable.

Background Requirements

According to official specifications published by Sri Lankan embassies, the background must be plain and light-colored — white, light blue, or light grey — and must clearly contrast with the applicant's face and clothing. The background must be entirely free of patterns, textures, gradients, furniture, or any other objects. Uniform lighting must be used to eliminate shadows on the background. Any shadow falling behind the subject will result in the photo being rejected.

Head Size, Position, and Framing

The photo must show a close-up of the head and partial shoulders. The face — measured from the forehead edge to the bottom of the chin — must occupy 70% to 80% of the total photo height, which corresponds to approximately 2.5 cm (25 mm) of the 45 mm image height. The head must be perfectly centered within the frame and positioned straight — no tilting, turning, or angling in any direction. The full face from chin to crown must be visible, and no part of the head should be cut off at any edge.

Eyes, Face, and Expression

Both eyes must be fully open, clearly visible, and looking directly into the camera. No hair should obscure the face, forehead, or ears. The eyes must not be affected by red-eye — photos with red-eye will not be accepted. A neutral facial expression with a closed mouth is required; smiling, frowning, raised eyebrows, squinting, or any exaggerated expression is prohibited. The face must be in sharp focus and free from any ink marks, creases, lines, or blemishes on the print.

Lighting and Skin Tone

The photo must have adequate, uniform brightness and contrast. The applicant's skin tone must appear natural — over-exposed photos where skin appears too light, and under-exposed photos where skin appears too dark, will not be accepted. Use diffused, even lighting from the front to avoid harsh shadows on the face, particularly around the nose, chin, and eye sockets. Flash photography should be handled carefully to prevent uneven lighting or glare.

Clothing and Accessories

Wear your everyday, regular clothing for the photo. There is no specific dress code, but clothing should clearly contrast with the background — avoid wearing all-white or very light-colored tops that may blend into the background. Uniforms or costumes that resemble official, military, or government attire are not permitted. Spectacles (glasses) are not accepted under any circumstances for Sri Lanka passport photos — this is an absolute rule stated in the official specifications, with no medical exceptions. Head coverings and head-dress are generally not allowed; however, religious or medical exceptions may apply provided the full face remains clearly visible from chin to forehead with no shadows cast on the face.

Digital Photo Submission Process

For in-country passport applications, the Department of Immigration and Emigration requires applicants to visit an authorized photo studio that is registered with the department's digital photo submission system. The studio captures your biometric photograph and transmits it electronically to the Department's computer system. The studio then issues a Studio Acknowledgement Note, which must be submitted with your application documents. No physical printed photographs are required for in-country applications processed at the Head Office or regional offices. Applicants applying through Sri Lankan Diplomatic Missions overseas may submit applications without a digital photograph, providing fingerprints upon their first arrival in Sri Lanka.

Photo Recency

Passport photos must have been taken within the last six months of your application date. Only current, recent photos that accurately represent your present appearance will be accepted. Photos that are older than six months will be rejected regardless of quality.

Children and Infants

All children, including newborns, must have their own individual Sri Lanka passport — inclusion of children in a parent's passport is no longer permitted. Children's passport photos must meet the same technical specifications as adult photos. The child must be the only subject in the frame, with no hands, toys, car seats, or other people visible. For infants, closed eyes may be tolerated given the difficulty of keeping very young children alert, but open eyes are always preferred.

Common Reasons for Rejection

The most frequent reasons Sri Lanka passport photos are rejected by the Department of Immigration and Emigration include: wearing spectacles, shadows on the face or background, incorrect head size proportions, photo older than six months, non-uniform or patterned background, red-eye effect, poor print quality (inkjet or Polaroid prints), digital retouching or editing, and head tilting or turning.
visacontent:
Sri Lanka Visa Photo Requirements (2026)

Applying for a Sri Lanka visa — whether through the Electronic Travel Authorization (ETA) system, a traditional visa application at a Sri Lankan embassy, or visa on arrival at Bandaranaike International Airport (BIA) — requires a compliant passport-style photograph. A non-compliant photo is one of the most common reasons for visa application delays and rejections. This complete guide covers all official Sri Lanka visa photo requirements for 2026 to help ensure your application is accepted the first time.

Photo Size and Dimensions

The Sri Lanka visa photo must measure exactly 35 mm × 45 mm (3.5 cm × 4.5 cm), the same standard biometric size used for the Sri Lanka passport. At 600 DPI, this equals approximately 413 × 531 pixels. Even minor deviations from these dimensions will result in automatic rejection — particularly for online ETA applications where the portal validates image dimensions programmatically. For digital submissions, the file must be in JPEG (JPG) format.

Background Requirements

The background must be plain white or very light-colored — white is the most recommended and universally accepted option. Light grey or cream backgrounds may be acceptable in some cases, but a pure white background eliminates any ambiguity. The background must be completely free of patterns, textures, objects, shadows, and gradients. Any colored, patterned, or busy background will result in immediate rejection. Ensure the background is evenly lit with no shadows cast by the subject behind them.

Face Size and Head Positioning

The face must be fully frontal, centered in the frame, and the head must occupy 70% to 80% of the total image height from the chin to the crown. The top of the shoulders should be visible at the bottom of the frame. The head must be straight — not tilted to either side, turned, or angled in any direction. Both ears should be visible. The full face from chin to forehead must appear within the frame with no cropping at any edge.

Facial Expression and Eyes

Maintain a neutral, relaxed facial expression — mouth closed, no smiling, no frowning, no raised or furrowed eyebrows. Both eyes must be fully open and looking directly at the camera. Eyebrows must not be raised or squinted. Red-eye in photos is not permitted; use appropriate lighting or red-eye correction during the shoot rather than digital post-processing. The face must be sharp, in focus, and clearly visible without any obstruction from hair, accessories, or shadows.

Photo Quality and Technical Specifications

The visa photo must be in full color — black-and-white images are not accepted. The image must be sharp, properly exposed, and free from pixelation, blur, or graininess. No digital filters, retouching, beauty enhancements, or color corrections are permitted. Lighting must be uniform across the entire face with no hot spots, harsh shadows, or uneven exposure. Natural skin tones must be accurately represented — over- or under-exposed skin tones will lead to rejection. For ETA online submissions, the file must be in JPEG format; file size requirements follow the ETA portal's validation rules, so ensure the image is high quality while staying within the upload limit. For physical visa applications at embassies or consulates, use professional-grade glossy or matte photo paper printed at an authorized photo studio.

Clothing and Accessories

Wear everyday clothing that clearly contrasts with the white background — avoid all-white or very light-colored tops as they can blend into the background and create compliance issues. Uniforms or clothing resembling military, official, or government attire are not permitted. Spectacles and glasses are strongly discouraged and should be removed for the photo. Sunglasses and tinted glasses are strictly prohibited under all circumstances. Head coverings are generally not permitted, with the exception of those worn for genuine religious reasons — in such cases, the full face from chin to forehead must remain clearly and fully visible with no shadows cast on the face. Avoid wearing heavy jewelry or large accessories that draw attention away from the face or cast shadows.

Sri Lanka ETA — Digital Photo Guidelines

The Sri Lanka Electronic Travel Authorization (ETA) is the primary visa pathway for most visitors in 2026 and is applied for online through the official Department of Immigration and Emigration portal. The ETA application requires uploading a passport-style color photograph in JPG format with a white background, neutral expression, and no glasses. The photo must accurately represent your current appearance. Poor quality photos or photos that do not meet the technical specifications are a leading cause of ETA processing delays. It is strongly recommended to have your photo professionally prepared and verified against the official requirements before uploading.

Photo Recency

All visa photos — whether for ETA, embassy applications, or visa on arrival — must have been taken within the last six months of the application or travel date. Photos older than six months will not be accepted. The photo must accurately reflect your current appearance, including current hair, any facial features, or significant changes in appearance since the photo was taken.

Children and Infants

Each child traveling to Sri Lanka, including newborns, must have their own individual visa application with a separate, individual photograph. The photo must show only the child — no hands, toys, car seats, supporting adults, or other objects should be visible. For infants, the same technical specifications apply; closed eyes may be tolerated for very young infants but open eyes are always preferred. The child's full face must be visible and centered in the frame.

Common Reasons for Sri Lanka Visa Photo Rejection

The most frequent reasons visa photos are rejected include: colored or patterned background, photo taken more than six months ago, wearing sunglasses or tinted glasses, digital editing or filters applied, shadows on the face or background, incorrect head-to-frame ratio, non-JPEG file format for online submissions, poor image resolution, smiling or non-neutral expression, and head tilted or turned away from the camera.

Quick Compliance Checklist

Before submitting your Sri Lanka visa application, verify the following: ✔ Photo size is exactly 35 × 45 mm ✔ Background is plain white or very light ✔ Head occupies 70–80% of image height ✔ Face is centered, straight, and fully visible ✔ Both eyes open, no red-eye ✔ Neutral expression, mouth closed ✔ No glasses, sunglasses, or non-religious head coverings ✔ Full color, no digital retouching or filters ✔ Photo taken within the last 6 months ✔ JPEG format for online ETA submission ✔ Clothing contrasts with the white background

name: Tajikistan Passport
passportcontent:
Tajikistan Passport Photo Requirements (2026)

Applying for or renewing a Tajikistan passport requires submitting photographs that strictly conform to the official biometric standards set by Tajikistan's passport authorities. Failing to meet even one requirement can result in your application being delayed or returned. This complete guide covers every official Tajikistan passport photo requirement for 2026 — from exact dimensions and background color to facial expression, clothing, and print quality.

Photo Size and Dimensions

The official Tajikistan passport photo size is 35 mm × 45 mm (3.5 cm × 4.5 cm), equivalent to approximately 1.37 × 1.77 inches. At a resolution of 600 DPI, this corresponds to 413 × 531 pixels. Applicants are required to submit four (4) identical photos with their passport application. Photos that do not conform precisely to these measurements will not be accepted. The photo must be printed on high-quality photo paper at a minimum resolution of 600 DPI — standard inkjet or laser prints are not suitable for submission.

Background Requirements

The background must be plain white with no shadows, patterns, gradients, or textures of any kind. The background must be uniformly lit and completely free of any objects, furniture, or other people. The applicant's clothing must contrast clearly with the white background — wearing white or very light-colored tops is strongly discouraged, as clothing that blends into the background can cause the photo to be rejected. A plain white wall or a clean white sheet (non-transparent) works well as a home background if a professional studio is unavailable.

Head Size and Positioning

The face — measured from the forehead edge to the bottom of the chin — must occupy 70% to 80% of the total photo height, with the head length from chin to forehead typically measuring between 31 mm and 36 mm within the 45 mm frame. The head must be perfectly centered within the photo. In the case of voluminous or large hairstyles, ensure that the entire head including the hairstyle is fully depicted within the frame without reducing the visible face size. The top of the shoulders must be visible at the lower portion of the image to provide proper framing context. The face must not be cut off at any edge of the photograph.

Facial Expression and Eyes

Maintain a neutral facial expression with your mouth closed. Smiling, frowning, raised eyebrows, squinting, or any non-neutral expression is not permitted. Both eyes must be fully open, clearly visible, and looking directly into the camera lens. There must be no hair, accessories, or shadows obscuring the eyes. Red-eye caused by camera flash must be avoided — use appropriate diffused lighting or an off-axis flash setup rather than post-processing corrections. The face must be in sharp focus with all facial features clearly distinguishable.

Lighting and Photo Quality

Lighting must be even and uniform across the entire face, with no harsh shadows on the cheeks, nose, chin, or forehead. Reflections on the face from direct flash must also be avoided. The photo must be in full color — black-and-white or monochrome photos are not accepted. The image must be sharp, properly exposed, and free from blur, pixelation, or graininess. No digital retouching, beauty filters, or color alterations are permitted. The skin tone must appear natural and true to life — both over-exposed (too light) and under-exposed (too dark) photos will be rejected.

Clothing and Accessories

Wear regular, everyday clothing for the photo. There is no specific dress code, but clothing must clearly contrast with the white background — avoid all-white or very light tops. Uniforms or clothing resembling military, police, or official government attire are not permitted. Headwear is generally not allowed; exceptions are made only for religious or cultural reasons, provided the full face remains clearly visible from chin to forehead with no shadows cast on the face. Glasses must be removed for Tajikistan passport photos — spectacles are not permitted regardless of prescription or medical need. Beards are permitted provided they represent the applicant's everyday appearance and do not significantly obscure facial features.

Photo Recency

Tajikistan passport photos must have been taken within the last six months of the application date. Photos older than six months are considered outdated and will not be accepted. The photo must accurately reflect your current appearance — significant changes in appearance (such as weight changes, new facial hair, or changed hairstyle) since the photo was taken may require a new photo to be submitted.

Infants and Children

Infants and children must have their own individual passport photo meeting the same technical specifications as adults. The photo must show only the child — no hands, toys, car seats, or other adults may appear in the frame. Photographing infants from above in a lying-down position is acceptable, provided a white sheet is placed underneath to achieve the required white background. The infant's headwear and pacifier must be removed before taking the photo. The child's full face must be visible and centered within the frame.

Common Reasons for Rejection

The most frequent reasons Tajikistan passport photos are rejected include: glasses not removed, shadows on the face or background, head not centered or tilted, photo older than six months, background that is not plain white, clothing that blends into the background, red-eye effect, digital retouching or filters applied, head or hairstyle cut off at the edges, and insufficient print resolution or quality.

Where to Apply

Tajikistan passport applications are processed through the Ministry of Internal Affairs (passportization departments) at regional offices across Tajikistan, as well as through Tajikistan diplomatic missions abroad. When applying at overseas embassies or consulates, four compliant printed photographs must accompany the application along with all required supporting documents.
visacontent:
Tajikistan Visa Photo Requirements (2026)

Tajikistan operates a three-tier visa system in 2026: visa-free access for select CIS nationals, an electronic visa (e-visa) system through the official portal at evisa.tj for most OECD and ASEAN nationals, and embassy-issued visas for all other nationalities. Importantly, the photo requirements differ between the Tajikistan passport and the Tajikistan e-visa — the e-visa uses a larger photo format. This guide covers all official photo requirements for both visa types to help ensure your application is accepted without delays.

Tajikistan E-Visa Photo Size — Important Difference

Unlike the passport photo, the Tajikistan e-visa photo has a different, larger format: 50 mm × 60 mm (5 cm × 6 cm), equivalent to approximately 1.97 × 2.36 inches. At 600 DPI this corresponds to 1181 × 1417 pixels. This is a critical distinction — submitting a 35×45 mm passport-format photo for the e-visa application may result in rejection at the evisa.tj portal. The digital file must be in JPEG format with a maximum file size of 240 KB and a minimum resolution of 300 DPI. Only one photo needs to be uploaded for the online e-visa application.

Embassy Visa Photo Size

For applicants applying for a Tajikistan visa in person at a Tajikistan embassy or consulate abroad, the standard 35 mm × 45 mm passport-format photo applies. Four printed photos are typically required alongside the completed visa application form and supporting documents including a valid passport, proof of accommodation, and (for some nationalities) an invitation letter or confirmed itinerary.

Background Requirements

For both e-visa and embassy visa applications, the background must be plain white — uniform, smooth, and entirely free of patterns, gradients, textures, shadows, or any other objects. The applicant's clothing must contrast clearly with the white background. Off-white, cream, grey, or any colored background will result in the photo being rejected. Ensure the background is evenly and uniformly lit to prevent shadows forming behind the subject.

Head Size and Facial Framing

The face — from the forehead edge to the bottom of the chin — must occupy 70% to 80% of the total image height. For the e-visa 50×60 mm format, the minimum head height is 45 mm within the 60 mm frame, with the top of the head positioned a minimum of 3.6 mm from the top edge of the photo. The head must be centered, straight, and looking directly forward. The full face from chin to crown must be visible within the frame with no cropping at any edge. The top of the shoulders must be visible at the bottom of the image.

Facial Expression and Eyes

A neutral, relaxed facial expression is required — mouth closed, no smiling, no frowning, no raised eyebrows. Both eyes must be fully open, clearly visible, and looking directly at the camera. Eyes must not be squinted, partially closed, or obscured by hair or accessories. Red-eye is not permitted — use diffused or off-camera lighting during the shoot to prevent red-eye rather than applying digital corrections afterward.

Photo Quality and Technical Standards

The photo must be in full color with natural, accurate skin tone representation. The image must be sharp, in focus, and free from blur, pixelation, or graininess. No digital retouching, beauty filters, brightness adjustments, or color corrections are permitted. Lighting must be uniform across the entire face — no harsh shadows on the cheeks, nose, chin, or forehead, and no reflective glare from direct flash. For the online e-visa upload, use JPEG format, ensure a minimum resolution of 300 DPI, and keep the file size under 240 KB.

Clothing and Accessories

Wear your regular, everyday clothing for the visa photo. Clothing must clearly contrast with the white background — avoid all-white or very light-colored tops that may blend into the background. Uniforms or attire resembling official, military, or government dress are not permitted. Glasses must be removed for the photo. Headwear is not permitted except for verified religious or cultural reasons, in which case the full face from chin to forehead must remain clearly visible with no shadows cast on any facial feature. Beards are acceptable if they represent the applicant's natural everyday appearance.

How to Apply for the Tajikistan E-Visa in 2026

The Tajikistan e-visa is applied for entirely online through the official portal at evisa.tj. The application requires uploading a compliant face photo (50×60 mm, JPEG, under 240 KB) and a scanned color copy of the passport data page. The processing fee is approximately USD 30 base fee plus USD 20 processing fee. Standard processing takes 1 to 3 working days. The e-visa is valid for 90 days from the date of issue, with a maximum stay of 60 days. Applicants are advised to print two copies of the approved e-visa to present at the border. The e-visa is accepted at all airports and designated land border crossings. Note: travelers intending to visit the Gorno-Badakhshan Autonomous Oblast (GBAO) — including the Pamir Highway — must also obtain a separate GBAO permit, which can be added to the e-visa application for an additional USD 20–30.

Photo Recency

Visa photos must have been taken within the last six months of the application date. This applies to both e-visa digital uploads and physical photos submitted at embassies. The photo must accurately reflect your current appearance at the time of application.

Common Reasons for Tajikistan Visa Photo Rejection

Frequent rejection reasons include: using a 35×45 mm photo for the e-visa instead of the required 50×60 mm format, file size exceeding 240 KB, non-JPEG file format, colored or non-white background, shadows on the face or background, wearing glasses, red-eye, digital retouching or filters, head tilted or not centered, photo older than six months, and clothing color blending into the white background.

Quick Compliance Checklist

Before submitting your Tajikistan visa application, verify: ✔ E-visa photo is 50×60 mm (not 35×45 mm) ✔ Embassy visa photo is 35×45 mm ✔ Background is plain white, no shadows ✔ Head occupies 70–80% of image height ✔ Face centered, straight, looking forward ✔ Both eyes open, no red-eye ✔ Neutral expression, mouth closed ✔ Glasses removed ✔ No non-religious headwear ✔ Full color, no retouching or filters ✔ Photo taken within 6 months ✔ JPEG format, under 240 KB, min 300 DPI for e-visa upload

name: Greece Passport

Here are the complete specifications for Greece passport photos. Ensure your photo meets all requirements to avoid processing delays.
